---
title: "Spatial Figure Making"
author: "Simon Van Deursen"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
# Sets root directory for chunks to the directory where the file is located
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Manuscripts/Wound Healing Atlas Paper/Immunome Paper/Figure 3 Spatial")

library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(cowplot)
library(scales)
library(viridis)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(writexl)
library(SeuratWrappers)
library(Banksy)
library(hdf5r)
library(spacexr)
library(sfarrow)
library(Rfast2)
library(rlang)
#install.packages("ggsignif")  # only once
library(ggsignif)
library(colorway)
library(khroma)
library(ggbreak)
library(extrafont)

#font_import() # Needed to import fonts from computer 
#Load needed functions
source("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/scRNA SEQ of wounds/Skin Wound Healing Atlas/Useful Functions/GGsave_multidevice.R")
source("~/Documents/Shared-Utils/Useful Functions/CustomSpatialHighlightPlot2.R")

# needs to be set for large dataset analysis
options(future.globals.maxSize = 1e9)

#Save a custom theme for ggplots, including setting Arial as default font and
#making background transparent
custom_theme <- theme(
  text = element_text(family = "Arial"),
  plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
  panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
  legend.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
  panel.grid = element_blank(),
  panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
  panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
)
```
Load in the datasets
```{r Load data}
object_uw <- readRDS("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Sequencing Repository/Users/Simon/Spatial Data/RDS Files/YWV01_UW_FineAnnotation_092525.rds")

object_d4 <- readRDS("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Sequencing Repository/Users/Simon/Spatial Data/RDS Files/YWV04_D4PW_FineAnnotation_092525.rds")

object_d7 <- readRDS("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Sequencing Repository/Users/Simon/Spatial Data/RDS Files/YWV03_D7PW_FineAnnotation_092525.rds")

object_d30 <- readRDS("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Sequencing Repository/Users/Simon/Spatial Data/RDS Files/YWV02_D30PW_FineAnnotation_092525.rds")
```

For ease of plotting, generate a list of objects
```{r Generate dataset list for plotting}
# List of all conditions with metadata
plot_info <- list(
  "YWV01_UW"   = list(object = object_uw,  regions = c("Region1", "Region2","ROI"), pt.size.factor = 1.2),
  "YWV04_D4PW" = list(object = object_d4, regions = c("Region1", "Region2","ROI1"),   pt.size.factor = 1.25),
  "YWV03_D7PW" = list(object = object_d7, regions = c("Region1", "Region2","ROI1"),   pt.size.factor = 1.25),
  "YWV02_D30PW"= list(object = object_d30, regions = c("Region1", "Region2","ROI"), pt.size.factor = 1)
)
```
Get counts of each region
```{r}
table(object_uw$Region)
table(object_d4$Region)
table(object_d7$Region)
table(object_d30$Region)
```

Save scatter plots of the relationship between nCount and nFeature
```{r Plot QC Scatter plot}
for(sample_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[sample_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  
  FeatureScatter(seurat_obj, feature1 = "nCount_Spatial", feature2 = "nFeature_Spatial",
               group.by = "orig.ident", cols = "grey25", raster = F) & custom_theme
  
  ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(sample_name,"nCount_nFeature_Scatter"), height = 7, width = 8)
}
```

```{r Loop to save plots of anatomical sites}
setwd("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Manuscripts/Wound Healing Atlas Paper/Immunome Paper/Figure 3 Spatial/Anatomical Sites")

anatomical_sites <- union(unique(object_d7$AnatomicalSite),unique(object_d30$AnatomicalSite))
# Set levels of anatomical sites
anatomical_sites <- factor(anatomical_sites, levels = sort(anatomical_sites))

anatomical_pal <- setNames(scCustomize::Hue_Pal(length(anatomical_sites)), levels(anatomical_sites)) 

# Loop over each object and its regions
for (sample_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[sample_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  regions <- info$regions
  
  for (region in regions) {
    p <- SpatialDimPlot(seurat_obj, group.by = "AnatomicalSite", images = region,
                        pt.size.factor = 3) &
      scale_fill_manual(values = anatomical_pal) &
      custom_theme +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

    ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(sample_name, "_AnatomicalSite_", region))
    
  }
}
```

```{r Loop to save plots of 10X graph clusters}
# Loop over each object and its regions
for (sample_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[sample_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  regions <- info$regions
  pt.size <- info$pt.size.factor
  cluster_levels <- unique(seurat_obj$alignment_clusters)
  cluster_colors <- setNames(viridis(length(cluster_levels), option = "H"), sort(cluster_levels))
  
  for (region in regions) {
    p <- SpatialDimPlot(seurat_obj, group.by = "alignment_clusters", images = region,
                        pt.size.factor = pt.size) &
      scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_colors) &
      custom_theme +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

    ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(sample_name, "_10XClustering_", region))
    
    p <- SpatialDimPlot(seurat_obj, group.by = "alignment_clusters", images = region,
                        pt.size.factor = pt.size, label = T, repel = T, label.size = 3) & 
      scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_colors) & 
      custom_theme +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
    
    ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(sample_name, "_10XClusteringLabeled_", region))
  }
}
```
We can also generate plots highlighting each cluster in its respective colors
```{r UW 10X clustering split plot}
Idents(object_uw) <- "alignment_clusters"
# Get cells grouped by metacluster
cells <- CellsByIdentities(object_uw)

full_pal <- setNames(viridis(length(levels(object_uw$alignment_clusters)),
                                     option = "H"), levels(object_uw$alignment_clusters)) 
# Build a full palette including “Unselected" - necessary to change background color
full_pal <- c(
  full_pal,
  Unselected = "grey65"
)

# Make SpatialDimPlot
p <- SpatialDimPlot(
  object_uw,
  images          = "slice1",
  cells.highlight = cells,
  cols.highlight  = full_pal,  # placeholder
  facet.highlight = TRUE,
  combine         = TRUE
) + NoLegend() & custom_theme

# Override the fill scale
p <- p & scale_fill_manual(
  values = full_pal,
  breaks = names(full_pal)  # ensures both the metacluster and “Unselected" levels are known
)

p & theme(title = element_text(face = "bold"),
          panel.grid = element_blank())

#ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = "YWV01_UW_10XClustersSplitDimPlot", width = 10, height = 15)
```
```{r Plot QC Scatter plot}
for(sample_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[sample_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  
  FeatureScatter(seurat_obj, feature1 = "nCount_Spatial", feature2 = "nFeature_Spatial",
               group.by = "orig.ident", cols = "grey25", raster = F) & custom_theme
  
  ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(sample_name,"nCount_nFeature_Scatter"), height = 7, width = 8)
}
```

```{r Loop to save plots of seurat clusters}
# Loop over each object and its regions
for (sample_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[sample_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  regions <- info$regions
  pt.size <- info$pt.size.factor
  cluster_levels <- levels(seurat_obj$seurat_cluster.projected)
  cluster_colors <- setNames(viridis(length(cluster_levels), option = "H"), cluster_levels)
  
  for (region in regions) {
    p <- SpatialDimPlot(seurat_obj, group.by = "seurat_cluster.projected", images = region) &
      scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_colors) &
      custom_theme +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

    ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(sample_name, "_SeuratClustering_", region))
    
    p <- SpatialDimPlot(seurat_obj, group.by = "seurat_cluster.projected", images = region,
                        label = T, repel = T, label.size = 3, pt.size.factor = pt.size) & 
      scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_colors) & 
      custom_theme +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
    
    ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(sample_name, "_SeuratClusteringLabeled_", region))
    
  }
}
```
```{r Loop to save plots of BANKSY clusters}
# Loop over each object and its regions
for (sample_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[sample_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  regions <- info$regions
  pt.size <- info$pt.size.factor
  cluster_levels <- levels(seurat_obj$banksy_cluster_0.5)
  cluster_colors <- setNames(viridis(length(cluster_levels), option = "H"), cluster_levels)
  
  for (region in regions) {
    p <- SpatialDimPlot(seurat_obj, group.by = "banksy_cluster_0.5", images = region) &
      scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_colors) &
      custom_theme +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

    ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(sample_name, "_BANKSYClustering_", region))
    
    p <- SpatialDimPlot(seurat_obj, group.by = "banksy_cluster_0.5", images = region,
                        label = T, repel = T, label.size = 3, pt.size.factor = pt.size) & 
      scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_colors) & 
      custom_theme +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
    
    ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(sample_name, "_BANKSYClusteringLabeled_", region))
    
  }
}
```
Similarly, we want to save plots of cell type annotations, both by cell type and
metacluster. We will start by saving defined palettes from each of these
```{r Save cell type color palette}
# Set color palette for main metacluster colors
metacluster_colors <- c(
  "IFE Keratinocytes" = "#364B9A",
  "HF Keratinocytes"  = "#56B4E9",
  "Sebocytes"         = "#8DD3C7",
  "Fibroblasts"       = "indianred2",
  "Immune cells"      = "#198F1B",
  "Endothelial cells" = "#F6C800",
  "Pericytes"         = "#D12E82",
  "Muscle cells"            = "orange2",
  "Schwann cells"     = "purple",
  "Melanocytes"       = "pink1",
  "Adipocytes"        = "deeppink",
  "Red Blood Cells"   = "#A7222B",
  "Unknown" = "grey70"
)
# Load fine cell type color palette
cell_colors <- readRDS("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Manuscripts/Wound Healing Atlas Paper/Hair Follicle Paper/Figure 1/color_palette.rds")
# Add Unknown to cell palettte
cell_colors <- c(cell_colors, "Unknown" = "white")
```

```{r Loop to save cell type and metacluster plots}
# Loop over each object and its regions, plotting cell types with associated palette
for (sample_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[sample_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  regions <- info$regions
  cluster_levels <- levels(seurat_obj$first_type)
  pt.size <- plot_info[[sample_name]]$pt.size.factor
  
  for (region in regions) {
    # Plot cell types unlabeled
    p <- SpatialDimPlot(seurat_obj, group.by = "first_type", images = region, pt.size.factor = pt.size) & 
      scale_fill_manual(values = cell_colors) & 
      custom_theme + NoLegend() +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
    
    ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(sample_name, "_RCTDCellType_", region))
    
    # Plot cell type labeled
    p <- SpatialDimPlot(seurat_obj, group.by = "first_type", images = region, 
                        pt.size.factor = pt.size, label = T, repel = T, label.size = 2) & 
      scale_fill_manual(values = cell_colors) & 
      custom_theme + NoLegend() +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
    
    ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(sample_name, "_RCTDCellTypeLabeled_", region))
    
    # Plot metaclusters for each region
    p <- SpatialDimPlot(seurat_obj, group.by = "metaclusters", images = region,
                        pt.size.factor = pt.size) & 
      scale_fill_manual(values = metacluster_colors) & 
      custom_theme + NoLegend() +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
    
    ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(sample_name, "_RCTDMetaclusters_", region))
  }
}
```
Save UMAP plots with the 10X clustering or metacluster annotations overlayed
```{r Save UMAPs}
# Loop to save annotated UMAPs
for(sample_name in names(plot_info)) {
  
  seurat_obj <- plot_info[[sample_name]]$object
  
  # 10X clustering 
  cluster_levels <- unique(seurat_obj$alignment_clusters)
  cluster_colors <- setNames(viridis(length(cluster_levels), option = "H"), sort(cluster_levels))
 
  p <- DimPlot(seurat_obj, reduction = "full.umap.sketch", group.by = "alignment_clusters",
               raster = F, cols = cluster_colors) + 
    labs(title = "") + 
    NoAxes() & 
    custom_theme
    
  p + NoLegend() 

  ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(sample_name,"10XClusterUMAP_NoLegend"), height = 7, width = 7)
  ggsave_multidevice(plot = p, filename_base = paste0(sample_name,"10XClusterUMAP_Legend"), height = 7, width = 8)
  
  # Seurat clustering 
  cluster_levels <- levels(seurat_obj$seurat_cluster.projected)
  cluster_colors <- setNames(viridis(length(cluster_levels), option = "H"), cluster_levels)
  
  p <- DimPlot(seurat_obj, reduction = "full.umap.sketch", group.by = "seurat_cluster.projected",
               raster = F, cols = cluster_colors) + 
    labs(title = "") + 
    NoAxes() & 
    custom_theme
    
  p + NoLegend() 

  ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(sample_name,"SeuratClusterUMAP_NoLegend"), height = 7, width = 7)
  ggsave_multidevice(plot = p, filename_base = paste0(sample_name,"SeuratClusterUMAP_Legend"), height = 7, width = 8)
  
  # BANKSY clustering
  cluster_levels <- levels(seurat_obj$banksy_cluster_0.8)
  cluster_colors <- setNames(viridis(length(cluster_levels), option = "H"), cluster_levels)
  
  p <- DimPlot(seurat_obj, reduction = "full.umap.sketch", group.by = "banksy_cluster_0.8",
               raster = F, cols = cluster_colors) + 
    labs(title = "") + 
    NoAxes() & 
    custom_theme
    
  p + NoLegend() 
  ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(sample_name,"BANKSYClusterUMAP_NoLegend"), height = 7, width = 7)
  ggsave_multidevice(plot = p, filename_base = paste0(sample_name,"BANKSYClusterUMAP_Legend"), height = 7, width = 8)
  
  # Repeat for metaclusters
  p <- DimPlot(seurat_obj, reduction = "full.umap.sketch", group.by = "metaclusters",
               raster = F, cols = metacluster_colors) + 
    labs(title = "") & 
    custom_theme
  
  p + NoLegend() + NoAxes() 

  ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(sample_name,"MetaclusterUMAP_NoLegend"), height = 7, width = 7)

  ggsave_multidevice(plot = p, filename_base = paste0(sample_name,"MetaclusterUMAP_Legend"), height = 7, width = 8)
}
```
We also need to save the fine cell type legend, which should be the same across all objects
```{r Save fine cell type legend}
p <- DimPlot(object_d7, cols = cell_colors, group.by = "first_type",
             reduction = "full.umap.sketch") + 
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 14)) & custom_theme

# Extract the legend using cowplot
library(cowplot)
legend <- get_legend(p)

ggsave("FineCellTypeLegend.png", plot = legend, width = 12, height = 5, 
       dpi = 300, bg = "transparent")
```
We are also interested in the quantification of the representation of celltypes
within each cluster
```{r Quantify metacluster representation by cluster}
# Quantify metacluster composition within defined clusters
quantify_metacluster_representation <- function(obj, sample_name, annotation_col) {
  meta <- obj@meta.data
  
  # Dynamically select the annotation column and metacluster column
  meta <- meta %>%
    dplyr::select(annotation = all_of(annotation_col),
                  metacluster = metaclusters) %>%
    filter(!is.na(annotation), !is.na(metacluster))
  
  # Count and normalize
  count_table <- meta %>%
    dplyr::count(annotation, metacluster, name = "count") %>%
    group_by(annotation) %>%
    mutate(proportion = count / sum(count)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(sample = sample_name)
  
  return(count_table)
}

# Define clustering columns of interest
clustering_cols <- c("alignment_clusters", "seurat_cluster.projected", "banksy_cluster_0.8")

# Generate all results for each clustering column
all_results_list <- map(clustering_cols, function(cluster_col) {
  
  # For each sample
  results_df <- map2_dfr(plot_info, names(plot_info), function(info, sample_name) {
    result <- quantify_metacluster_representation(info$object, sample_name, annotation_col = cluster_col)
    
    # Optional: generate a barplot for each sample & clustering
    p <- ggplot(result, aes(x = annotation, y = proportion, fill = metacluster)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
      theme_minimal() +
      labs(title = paste(sample_name, "-", cluster_col),
           x = "Cluster", y = "Proportion", fill = "Metacluster") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = metacluster_colors) +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 12),
            axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
            axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, angle = 45),
            axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14)) +
      custom_theme + NoLegend()
    
    ggsave_multidevice(
      filename_base = paste0(sample_name, "_", cluster_col, "_MetaclusterCluster_Barplot"),
      plot = p, width = 8, height = 5
    )
    
    return(result)
  })
  
  return(results_df)
})

names(all_results_list) <- clustering_cols

# Export to Excel
write_xlsx(all_results_list, path = "Metacluster_Cluster_Representation.xlsx")
```
Similarly, we want to compare cell type counts and proportions across samples
```{r Compare metacluster counts and props across objects}
get_metacluster_counts <- function(seurat_obj, annotation_col, label, scaling_factor = 10000) {
  # Count raw metacluster values by Region
  count_data <- seurat_obj@meta.data %>%
    dplyr::count(Region, !!sym(annotation_col)) %>%
    group_by(Region) %>%
    mutate(
      Normalized = n / sum(n) * scaling_factor,
      Proportion = n / sum(n) * 100
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(Object = label)
  
  # Average across regions
  summary_data <- count_data %>%
    group_by(!!sym(annotation_col), Object) %>%
    summarise(
      Normalized = mean(Normalized),
      Proportion = mean(Proportion),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  # Return both in a named list
  return(list(
    region_counts = count_data,
    summary = summary_data
  ))
}

# Extract from each object
plot_data <- bind_rows(
  get_metacluster_counts(object_uw, annotation_col = "metaclusters", "Unwounded")$summary,
  get_metacluster_counts(object_d4, annotation_col = "metaclusters", "D4PW")$summary,
  get_metacluster_counts(object_d7, annotation_col = "metaclusters", "D7PW")$summary,
  get_metacluster_counts(object_d30, annotation_col = "metaclusters", "D30PW")$summary
)

# Get separator positions for vertical dashed lines
object_levels <- c("Unwounded","D4PW","D7PW","D30PW")
plot_data$Object <- factor(plot_data$Object, levels = object_levels)
separator_positions <- seq(1.5, length(object_levels) - 0.5, by = 1)

# Plot 1: Normalized bar plot (dodge)
p1 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Object, y = Normalized, fill = metaclusters)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = metacluster_colors) +
  labs(y = "Cell Count per 10,000 Cells",
       title = "Normalized Metacluster Counts") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = separator_positions, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, hjust = 0.5)
  )

# Plot 2: Stacked bar plot with proportions
p2 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Object, y = Proportion, fill = metaclusters)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = metacluster_colors) +
  labs(y = "Proportion (%)",
       title = "Average Metacluster Proportions per Object") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = separator_positions, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, angle = 90),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, hjust = 0.5)
  )

# Print plots
print(p1)
print(p2)

ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = "MetaclusterComparison_CountPlot", plot = p1,
                   width = 10, height = 8)
ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = "MetaclusterComparison_PropPlot", plot = p2,
                   width = 6, height = 5)
```
Utilizing normalized mutual information, we can compare the relationships between
different clusterings and the cell type annotations. NMI is a metric which quantifies
the relationships between two clustering methods to determine how much they agree,
with 0 being completely different and 1 being completely identical. By comparing
these values across the clustering methods used, we can determine how much one clustering
method is grouping cell types together.
```{r Run NMI on spatial clusterings vs cell types}
if (!requireNamespace("aricode")) install.packages("aricode")
library(aricode)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Define NMI calculation function for one object
calculate_nmi <- function(seurat_obj, sample_name) {
  # Extract vectors
  cluster_methods <- c("alignment_clusters", "banksy_cluster_0.5", "seurat_cluster.projected")
  cell_type_vector <- as.character(seurat_obj$first_type)
  
  nmi_scores <- sapply(cluster_methods, function(method) {
    cluster_vector <- as.character(seurat_obj[[method]][,1])  # pull as vector
    NMI(cluster_vector, cell_type_vector)
  })
  
  data.frame(
    Sample = sample_name,
    Method = c("10X Graph Clusters", "BANKSY Clusters", "Seurat Clusters"),
    NMI = as.numeric(nmi_scores)
  )
}

# Run NMI calculation for all samples in plot_info
nmi_all <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(plot_info), function(name) {
  obj <- plot_info[[name]]$object
  calculate_nmi(obj, name)
}))

nmi_all$Sample <- factor(nmi_all$Sample, levels = c("YWV01_UW","YWV04_D4PW","YWV03_D7PW","YWV02_D30PW"))
# Plot
ggplot(nmi_all, aes(x = Sample, y = NMI, fill = Method)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(NMI, 3)), 
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  labs(title = "NMI between Clustering Methods and Cell Type Annotations",
       x = "Dataset", y = "Normalized Mutual Information") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2")
ggsave("ClusteringAnnotationNMI_Barplot.png", width = 8, height = 5)
```

## Cell type quantification
### By Fine cell type
Compare cell type counts across conditions, averaging across regions
```{r Define a function to calculate raw and normalized cell counts and proportions}
calculate_and_plot <- function(object, source_label, annotation_column) {
  
  metadata <- object@meta.data
  annotation_sym <- sym(annotation_column)
  
  # Total cells per region
  total_cells_per_region <- metadata %>%
    group_by(Region) %>%
    summarize(Total_Cells = n(), .groups = "drop")

  avg_total_cells <- mean(total_cells_per_region$Total_Cells)

  # Count, normalize, and compute Per_10k_Cells
  region_counts <- metadata %>%
    group_by(.data[[annotation_column]], Region) %>%
    summarize(Count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    left_join(total_cells_per_region, by = "Region") %>%
    mutate(
      Proportion = (Count / Total_Cells) * 100,
      Normalized_Count = (Count / Total_Cells) * avg_total_cells, # scales to the average size of both regions in an object
      Per_10k_Cells = (Count / Total_Cells) * 10000 # scales to a set value for comparison across objects
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Compute mean and SEM per cell type for error bars
  summary_stats <- region_counts %>%
    group_by(.data[[annotation_column]]) %>%
    summarize(
      Avg_Proportion = mean(Proportion),
      Mean_Normalized = mean(Normalized_Count),
      SEM_Normalized = sd(Normalized_Count) / sqrt(n()),
      Mean_Per10k = mean(Per_10k_Cells),
      SEM_Per10k = sd(Per_10k_Cells) / sqrt(n()),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(Source = source_label)

  # Plots
  ## Plot of raw cell count by region
  count_plot <- ggplot(region_counts, aes(x = !!annotation_sym, y = Count, fill = Region)) +
    geom_col(position = position_dodge()) +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(
      title = paste("Cell Counts by Region -", source_label),
      x = "Cell Type", y = "Count"
    ) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  ## Plot of proportion
  proportion_plot <- ggplot(summary_stats, aes(x = Source, y = Avg_Proportion, fill = !!annotation_sym)) +
    geom_col(position = "stack", color = "black") +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(title = paste("Stacked Average Cell Type Proportions -", source_label),
         x = "Source", y = "Average Proportion (%)", fill = "Cell Type")
  
  ## Cell counts normalized to average region count
  normalized_count_plot <- ggplot(region_counts, aes(x = !!annotation_sym, y = Normalized_Count, fill = Region)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(
      title = paste("Normalized Cell Counts by Region -", source_label),
      x = "Cell Type", y = "Normalized Count"
    ) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  ## Average normalized counts with SEM
  mean_normalized_count_plot <- ggplot(summary_stats, aes(x = !!annotation_sym, y = Mean_Normalized, fill = !!annotation_sym)) +
    geom_col(color = "black") +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Mean_Normalized - SEM_Normalized,
                      ymax = Mean_Normalized + SEM_Normalized),
                  width = 0.3) +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(title = paste("Normalized Cell Counts (Mean ± SEM) -", source_label),
         x = "Cell Type", y = "Mean Normalized Count") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

  ## Normalized to 10k cells with SEM
  mean_per_10k_plot <- ggplot(summary_stats, aes(x = !!annotation_sym, y = Mean_Per10k, fill = !!annotation_sym)) +
    geom_col(color = "black") +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Mean_Per10k - SEM_Per10k,
                      ymax = Mean_Per10k + SEM_Per10k),
                  width = 0.3) +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(title = paste("Mean Count per 10k Cells (± SEM) -", source_label),
         x = "Cell Type", y = "Cell Count per 10,000 Cells") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

  return(list(
    region_counts = region_counts,
    summary_stats = summary_stats,
    avg_total_cells = avg_total_cells,
    plots = list(
      count_plot = count_plot,
      proportion_plot = proportion_plot,
      normalized_count_plot = normalized_count_plot,
      mean_normalized_count_plot = mean_normalized_count_plot,
      mean_per_10k_plot = mean_per_10k_plot
    )
  ))
}
```
With the function defined, we can apply it to the object and save the results as an excel file
```{r Quantify fine cell types using function and save results}
# Use lapply to apply the above function to each Visium object
results_list <- lapply(names(plot_info), function(dataset) {
  info <- plot_info[[dataset]]
  
  # Run the function
  result_object <- calculate_and_plot(
    object = info$object,
    source_label = dataset,
    annotation_column = "first_type"
  )
  
  # Save Excel file
  writexl::write_xlsx(
    list(
      "Region Counts" = result_object$region_counts,
      "Summary Data" = result_object$summary_stats
    ),
    paste0(dataset, "_FineCellTypeQuantification.xlsx")
  )
  
  # Return the result to keep in memory as well
  return(result_object)
})

# Name the list with dataset names
names(results_list) <- names(plot_info)
```

```{r Iterate over the stored plots to save fine cell type quantification by region}
# Define custom plot scales and colors for each dataset
plot_scales <- list(
  "YWV01_UW" = list(
    colors = c("navyblue","lightskyblue"),
    y_break = list(breaks = c(5000,15000), scales = 0.25, ticklabels = c(15000, 20000))
  ),
  "YWV04_D4PW" = list(
    colors = c("firebrick","lightcoral"),
    y_break = list(breaks = c(6500,9000), scales = 0.25, ticklabels = c(10000,12000))
  ),
  "YWV03_D7PW" = list(
    colors = c("darkorange3","orange"),
    y_break = list(breaks = c(10000,20000), scales = 0.25, ticklabels = c(20000, 25000))
  ),
  "YWV02_D30PW" = list(
    colors = c("goldenrod3","khaki"),
    y_break = list(breaks = c(6000,12000), scales = 0.25, ticklabels = c(12000, 15000))
  )
)

# Iterate through results_list and save plots
for(dataset in names(results_list)){
  res <- results_list[[dataset]]
  scales <- plot_scales[[dataset]]
  
  p <- res$plots$normalized_count_plot + 
      scale_fill_manual(values = scales$colors) + 
      scale_y_break(
        breaks = scales$y_break$breaks, 
        scales = scales$y_break$scales, 
        ticklabels = scales$y_break$ticklabels) +
      theme(
      text = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5),
      axis.title.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.75)
    ) + custom_theme 
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0(dataset, "_NormalizedCountsByRegion.png"), 
         plot = p, width = 15, height = 6, dpi = 300)
}
```
We can repeat the same process for other plots, including the mean cell counts per 10k cells
```{r Save plots of mean cell counts per 10k cells}
# Define custom plot scales and colors for each dataset
plot_scales <- list(
  "YWV01_UW" = list(breaks = c(875, 3000), scales = 0.1, ticklabels = c(3000,3500)),
  "YWV04_D4PW" = list(breaks = c(1250, 1750), scales = 0.1, ticklabels = c(1800,2200)),
  "YWV03_D7PW" = list(breaks = c(1100, 1750), scales = 0.1, ticklabels = c(2000,2600)),
  "YWV02_D30PW" = list(breaks = c(800, 1750), scales = 0.1, ticklabels = c(1800, 2200))
)

# Iterate through results_list and save plots
for(dataset in names(results_list)){
  res <- results_list[[dataset]]
  scales <- plot_scales[[dataset]]
  
  p <- res$plots$mean_per_10k_plot +                          
      scale_fill_manual(values = cell_colors) +
      scale_y_break(
        breaks = scales$breaks, 
        scales = scales$scales, 
        ticklabels = scales$ticklabels,
        expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.05))) +
      theme(
        text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.4),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16, vjust = 1.5),
        axis.text.y.right = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y.right = element_blank()
      ) + NoLegend() + custom_theme 
  
  ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(dataset, "_CellCountsper10kBarplot"), 
         plot = p, width = 15, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  
 p <- res$plots$mean_per_10k_plot +                          
  scale_fill_manual(values = cell_colors) +
      scale_y_break(
        breaks = scales$breaks, 
        scales = scales$scales, 
        ticklabels = scales$ticklabels,
        expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.05))) +
   coord_flip() +
 theme(
    text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.4),
    axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 1, size = 14, angle = 45),
    axis.text.x.top = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x.top = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, angle = 0),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y.right = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y.right = element_blank()
  ) + 
   NoLegend() + custom_theme 
 
ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(dataset, "_CellCountsper10kBarplot_Vertical"),
                  plot = p, width = 8, height = 18)
}
```

### By Metacluster
```{r Quantify metaclusters and save results}
# Use lapply to apply the above function to each Visium object
results_list <- lapply(names(plot_info), function(dataset) {
  info <- plot_info[[dataset]]
  
  # Run the function
  result_object <- calculate_and_plot(
    object = info$object,
    source_label = dataset,
    annotation_column = "metaclusters"
  )
  
  # Save Excel file
  writexl::write_xlsx(
    list(
      "Region Counts" = result_object$region_counts,
      "Summary Data" = result_object$summary_stats
    ),
    paste0(dataset, "_MetaclusterQuantification.xlsx")
  )
  
  # Return the result to keep in memory as well
  return(result_object)
})

# Name the list with dataset names
names(results_list) <- names(plot_info)
```

```{r Save plots of mean cell counts per 10k cells}
# Define custom plot scales and colors for each dataset
plot_scales <- list(
  "YWV01_UW" = list(breaks = c(2250, 3000), scales = 0.1, ticklabels = c(3500)),
  "YWV04_D4PW" = list(breaks = c(2000, 4750), scales = 0.1, ticklabels = c(5000)),
  "YWV03_D7PW" = list(breaks = c(2000, 4500), scales = 0.1, ticklabels = c(5500)),
  "YWV02_D30PW" = list(breaks = c(800, 1750), scales = 0.1, ticklabels = c(1800, 2200))
)

# Iterate through results_list and save plots
for(dataset in names(results_list)){
  res <- results_list[[dataset]]
  scales <- plot_scales[[dataset]]
  
  p <- res$plots$mean_per_10k_plot +                          
      scale_fill_manual(values = metacluster_colors) +
      scale_y_break(
        breaks = scales$breaks, 
        scales = scales$scales, 
        ticklabels = scales$ticklabels,
        expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.05))) +
      theme(
        text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.4),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 14),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16, vjust = 1.5),
        axis.text.y.right = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y.right = element_blank()
      ) + NoLegend() + custom_theme 
  
  ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(dataset, "_MetaclusterCountsper10kBarplot"), 
         plot = p, width = 15, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  
 p <- res$plots$mean_per_10k_plot +                          
  scale_fill_manual(values = metacluster_colors) +
      scale_y_break(
        breaks = scales$breaks, 
        scales = scales$scales, 
        ticklabels = scales$ticklabels,
        expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.05))) +
   coord_flip() +
 theme(
    text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.4),
    axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 1, size = 14, angle = 45),
    axis.text.x.top = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x.top = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16, angle = 0),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y.right = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y.right = element_blank()
  ) + 
   NoLegend() + custom_theme 
 
ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(dataset, "_MetaclusterCountsper10kBarplot_Vertical"),
                  plot = p, width = 8, height = 18)
}
```

```{r Plot averaged normalized metacluster counts per 10k cells}
# Plot the averaged cell count per 10k cells
results_list$YWV02_D30PW$plots$mean_per_10k_plot +                          
  scale_fill_manual(values = metacluster_colors) +
# scale_y_break(c(2000, 4500), scales = 0.1, ticklabels = c(5500), expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.05))) + # Edit values as needed
#  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,3500)) +
  theme(
    text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.4),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 14),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, vjust = 1.5),
    axis.text.y.right = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y.right = element_blank()
  ) + NoLegend() + custom_theme 

  ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(dataset, "_MetaclusterCountsper10kBarplot"),
                   width = 12, height = 8)

# Plot the same plot, pivoted to vertical
result_object$plots$per_10k_plot +                          
  scale_fill_manual(values = metacluster_colors) +
#  scale_y_break(c(2000, 3000), scales = 0.1, ticklabels = c(3000, 3500)) + # Edit values as needed
#  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,3500)) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.4),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 16),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y.right = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y.right = element_blank()
  ) + NoLegend() + custom_theme 

ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(dataset, "_MetaclusterCounts10kBarplot_Vertical"),
                   width = 8, height = 12)

# Plot the averaged proportions as a stacked barplot
results_list$YWV02_D30PW$plots$proportion_plot +                          
  scale_fill_manual(values = metacluster_colors) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = 0.1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(add = 0.1)) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme(
    text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, hjust = 0.4),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.y.right = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y.right = element_blank()
  ) + NoLegend() + custom_theme 
dggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(dataset, "_MetaclusterProportions"),
                   width = 6, height = 12)
```
### Quantify cell type representation across anatomical sites
First we will quantify representation of immune cells in unwounded tissues
```{r Quantify immune cell representation across anatomical sites in UW}
final_df <- object_uw@meta.data %>%
  group_by(Region, AnatomicalSite, first_type) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = "drop_last") %>%
  mutate(SiteTotal = sum(Count)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Region) %>%
  mutate(RegionTotal = sum(Count)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Add total count across both regions for each cell type in an anatomical site
  group_by(AnatomicalSite, first_type) %>%
  mutate(TotalAcrossRegions = sum(Count)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Calculate proportions and normalized counts
  mutate(
    PropWithinSite     = Count / SiteTotal,
    PropWithinRegion   = Count / RegionTotal,
    CountPer1000Spots  = (Count / RegionTotal) * 1000
  )

immune_df <- final_df %>%
  filter(first_type %in% immune_types) %>%
  mutate(first_type = droplevels(first_type)) %>%  # remove non-immune levels
  group_by(AnatomicalSite, first_type) %>%
  summarise(CountPer1000Spots = sum(CountPer1000Spots), .groups = "drop") %>%
  complete(AnatomicalSite, first_type, fill = list(CountPer1000Spots = 0))  %>%
  # remove endothelial
  filter(AnatomicalSite != "Endothelial") %>%
  mutate(AnatomicalSite = factor(
    AnatomicalSite, 
    levels = rev(c("Epidermis","Dermis","HF","Adipose","Muscle","Fascia"))
  ))

shortnames <- c("Monocytes I" = "Mono I",
            "Monocytes II" = "Mono II",
            "Monocytes III" = "Mono III",
            "Macrophages I" = "Mac I",
            "Macrophages II" = "Mac II",
            "Macrophages III" = "Mac III",
            "Macrophages IV" = "Mac IV",
            "Cycling Macrophages" = "Cycling Mac",
            "OC-like cells" = "OC-like",
            "moDCs" = "moDCs",
            "cDC1" = "cDC1",
            "cDC2" = "cDC2",
            "Migratory DCs" = "migDCs",
            "Langerhans cells" = "LCs",
            "pDCs" = "pDCs",
            "Neutrophils I" = "Neu I",
            "Neutrophils II" = "Neu II",
            "Neutrophils III" = "Neu III",
            "Basophils" = "Baso",
            "Mast cells" = "Mast",
            "NK cells" = "NK",
            "γδT cells" = "γδT",
            "T cells" = "T cells",
            "Tregs" = "Tregs",
            "ILCs" = "ILCs",
            "B cells" = "B cells")
# Rename immune celltypes to shorter versions

immune_df <- immune_df %>%
  mutate(first_type = recode(first_type, !!!shortnames))

p <- ggplot(immune_df, aes(x = first_type, y = AnatomicalSite, fill = CountPer1000Spots)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_viridis(option = "E", transform = "log1p", direction = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  custom_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 6),  # text size of legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8)
  ) +
  labs(
    fill = "Cells per 1000 spots (log1p)"
  ) 
p
ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = "YWV01_UW_AnatomicalSite_ImmuneHeatmap", plot = p,
                   width = 8, height = 2)
```
We can then take the other objects and use UW as a baseline to compare against
```{r}
# Parameters
ref_condition <- "YWV01_UW"  
plot_conditions <- setdiff(names(plot_info), ref_condition)
anatomical_levels <- rev(c("Epidermis","Dermis","HF","Adipose","Muscle","Fascia"))
facet_order <- c("YWV04_D4PW", "YWV03_D7PW", "YWV02_D30PW")

immune_list <- lapply(names(plot_info), function(cond) {
  obj <- plot_info[[cond]]$object
  
  obj@meta.data %>%
    filter(first_type %in% immune_types) %>%
    mutate(first_type = droplevels(first_type)) %>%
    group_by(Region, AnatomicalSite, first_type) %>%
    summarise(Count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(RegionTotal = sum(Count),
           CountPer1000Spots = (Count / RegionTotal) * 1000) %>%  # keep original scale
    group_by(AnatomicalSite, first_type) %>%
    summarise(CountPer1000Spots = sum(CountPer1000Spots), .groups = "drop") %>%
    complete(AnatomicalSite, first_type, fill = list(CountPer1000Spots = 0)) %>%
    filter(AnatomicalSite %in% anatomical_levels) %>%
    filter(AnatomicalSite != "Endothelial") %>%
    mutate(
      AnatomicalSite = factor(AnatomicalSite, levels = anatomical_levels),
      first_type = recode(first_type, !!!shortnames),
      Condition = cond
    )
})

immune_df_all <- bind_rows(immune_list)

baseline_df <- immune_df_all %>% 
  filter(Condition == ref_condition) %>%
  select(AnatomicalSite, first_type, Baseline = CountPer1000Spots)

immune_fc_A <- immune_df_all %>%
  filter(Condition %in% plot_conditions) %>%
  left_join(baseline_df, by = c("AnatomicalSite", "first_type")) %>%
  mutate(FoldChange = (CountPer1000Spots + 1) / (Baseline + 1),
         FoldChange = pmin(pmax(FoldChange, 0.1), 10),
         Condition = factor(Condition, levels = facet_order))

p_A <- ggplot(immune_fc_A, aes(x = first_type, y = AnatomicalSite, fill = FoldChange)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red",
                       midpoint = 1, limits = c(0.1, 10),
                       name = "Fold change vs UW") +
  facet_wrap(~Condition, ncol = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  custom_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
        axis.title = element_blank(),
        legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 8))

immune_fc_B <- immune_fc_A %>%
  group_by(Condition, AnatomicalSite) %>%
  mutate(PropWithinSite = CountPer1000Spots / sum(CountPer1000Spots)) %>%
  left_join(
    baseline_df %>%
      group_by(AnatomicalSite) %>%
      mutate(BaselineProp = Baseline / sum(Baseline)) %>%
      select(AnatomicalSite, first_type, BaselineProp),
    by = c("AnatomicalSite", "first_type")
  ) %>%
  mutate(FoldChange = (PropWithinSite + 1e-3) / (BaselineProp + 1e-3),
         FoldChange = pmin(pmax(FoldChange, 0.1), 10))

p_B <- ggplot(immune_fc_B, aes(x = first_type, y = AnatomicalSite, fill = FoldChange)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red",
                       midpoint = 1, limits = c(0.1, 10),
                       name = "Fold change vs UW") +
  facet_wrap(~Condition, ncol = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  custom_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
        axis.title = element_blank(),
        legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 8))

# Display
p_A  # Absolute abundance fold change
p_B  # Within-anatomical site composition fold change

# Save
ggsave_multidevice("ST_Immune_Abundance_FCHeatmap", plot = p_A, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave_multidevice("ST_Immune_Composition_FCHeatmap", plot = p_B, width = 8, height = 6)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ----- Generate summarized data (same as your baseline code) -----
immune_list <- lapply(names(plot_info), function(cond) {
  obj <- plot_info[[cond]]$object
  
  obj@meta.data %>%
    filter(first_type %in% immune_types) %>%
    mutate(first_type = droplevels(first_type)) %>%
    group_by(Region, AnatomicalSite, first_type) %>%
    summarise(Count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(RegionTotal = sum(Count),
           CountPer1000Spots = (Count / RegionTotal) * 1000) %>%
    group_by(AnatomicalSite, first_type) %>%
    summarise(CountPer1000Spots = sum(CountPer1000Spots), .groups = "drop") %>%
    complete(AnatomicalSite, first_type, fill = list(CountPer1000Spots = 0)) %>%
    filter(AnatomicalSite %in% anatomical_levels, AnatomicalSite != "Endothelial") %>%
    mutate(
      AnatomicalSite = factor(AnatomicalSite, levels = anatomical_levels),
      first_type = recode(first_type, !!!shortnames),
      Condition = cond
    )
})
immune_df_all <- bind_rows(immune_list)

# ----- Compute baseline and fold-change -----
baseline_df <- immune_df_all %>%
  filter(Condition == ref_condition) %>%
  select(AnatomicalSite, first_type, Baseline = CountPer1000Spots)

immune_fc <- immune_df_all %>%
  filter(Condition %in% plot_conditions) %>%
  left_join(baseline_df, by = c("AnatomicalSite", "first_type")) %>%
  mutate(FoldChange = (CountPer1000Spots + 1) / (Baseline + 1),
         FoldChange = pmin(pmax(FoldChange, 0.1), 10),
         Condition = factor(Condition, levels = facet_order))

# ----- Statistical testing (Wilcoxon) -----
test_df <- immune_df_all %>%
  filter(Condition %in% c(ref_condition, plot_conditions)) %>%
  group_by(AnatomicalSite, first_type, Condition) %>%
  summarise(values = CountPer1000Spots, .groups = "drop") %>%  # remove list()
  group_by(AnatomicalSite, first_type) %>%
  summarise(
    stats = list({
      conds <- split(values, Condition)
      lapply(setdiff(names(conds), ref_condition), function(tp) {
        if(length(conds[[tp]]) > 0 && length(conds[[ref_condition]]) > 0){
          p <- wilcox.test(conds[[tp]], conds[[ref_condition]])$p.value
        } else { p <- NA }
        data.frame(Condition=tp, p_val=p)
      }) %>% bind_rows()
    }), .groups="drop"
  ) %>%
  unnest(stats) %>%
  group_by(AnatomicalSite, first_type) %>%
  mutate(
    p_adj = p.adjust(p_val, method = "BH"),
    sig = case_when(
      p_adj <= 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_adj <= 0.01  ~ "**",
      p_adj <= 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    )
  )

# Join p-values with FC data
immune_fc <- immune_fc %>%
  left_join(test_df %>% select(AnatomicalSite, first_type, Condition, sig),
            by = c("AnatomicalSite","first_type","Condition"))

# ----- Option A Plot -----
p_A <- ggplot(immune_fc, aes(x = first_type, y = AnatomicalSite, fill = FoldChange)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sig), color = "black", size = 2.5) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red",
                       midpoint = 1, limits = c(0.1, 10),
                       name = "Fold change vs UW") +
  facet_wrap(~Condition, ncol = 1) +
  theme_minimal() + custom_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
        axis.title = element_blank())

# ----- Option B -----
immune_fc_B <- immune_fc %>%
  group_by(Condition, AnatomicalSite) %>%
  mutate(PropWithinSite = CountPer1000Spots / sum(CountPer1000Spots)) %>%
  left_join(
    baseline_df %>%
      group_by(AnatomicalSite) %>%
      mutate(BaselineProp = Baseline / sum(Baseline)) %>%
      select(AnatomicalSite, first_type, BaselineProp),
    by = c("AnatomicalSite", "first_type")
  ) %>%
  mutate(FoldChange = (PropWithinSite + 1e-3) / (BaselineProp + 1e-3),
         FoldChange = pmin(pmax(FoldChange, 0.1), 10))

p_B <- ggplot(immune_fc_B, aes(x = first_type, y = AnatomicalSite, fill = FoldChange)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sig), color = "black", size = 2.5) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red",
                       midpoint = 1, limits = c(0.1, 10),
                       name = "Fold change vs UW") +
  facet_wrap(~Condition, ncol = 1) +
  theme_minimal() + custom_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
        axis.title = element_blank())


p_A
p_B
```

## Highlight cell types of interest
### Highlight non-regenerated cell types
Now with our function defined, we can save plots for a specific cell type of interest.
For figures, we want to show that muscle and adipose tissue are not regenerated, 
so generate highlight plots showing just those populations.
```{r Save highlight plots of muscle and adipose tissue}
# For these plots, we need to add a color for Unselected cells to the palette
metacluster_colors <- c(metacluster_colors, Unselected = "white")

# We can test the function to ensure it looks good
p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
  seurat_obj = plot_info[["YWV03_D7PW"]]$object,
  region = "Region1",
  annotation_col = "metaclusters",
  cells_highlight = c("Muscle cells","Adipocytes"),
  pt.size.highlight = 1,
  pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
  stroke.size = 0.05,
  pal = metacluster_colors,
  custom_theme = custom_theme
) + NoLegend()
print(p)

# Run the function in a loop to save all regions and timepoints
for (info_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[info_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  regions <- info$regions
  
  Idents(seurat_obj) <- "metaclusters"
  
  for (region in regions) {
    # Generate the plot using the custom function
    p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
      seurat_obj = seurat_obj,
      region = region,
      annotation_col = "metaclusters",
      cells_highlight = c("Muscle cells","Adipocytes"),
      pt.size.highlight = 1,
      pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
      stroke.size = 0.05,
      pal = metacluster_colors,
      custom_theme = custom_theme
    ) +
      custom_theme +
      theme(
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
      ) +
      NoLegend()
    
    # Save plot
    ggsave_multidevice(
      filename_base = paste(info_name, region, "MuscleAdiposeHighlight", sep = "_"),
      plot = p,
      width = 7,
      height = 7
    )
  }
}
```
### Highlight immune cells
Highlight immune cells in each object using matched color palette to immunome atlas
```{r Define cell types to highlight and the color palette to use}
# Specify immune cell types
immune_types <- c("Monocytes I","Monocytes II","Monocytes III","Macrophages I",
                  "Macrophages II","Macrophages III","Macrophages IV",
                  "Cycling Macrophages","OC-like cells","moDCs","cDC1","cDC2",
                  "Migratory DCs","Langerhans cells","pDCs","Neutrophils I",
                  "Neutrophils II","Neutrophils III","Basophils","Mast cells",
                  "NK cells","γδT cells","T cells","Tregs","ILCs","B cells")

# Define palette for immune types
pal <- setNames(color("smoothrainbow")(length(immune_types), range = c(0.1, 0.9)),
                immune_types) 
# Add value for unselected cells, this is necessary for highlight plots
pal <- c(pal, Unselected = "white")
```

```{r Test custom highlight plot with immune cells}
p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
  seurat_obj = plot_info[["YWV03_D7PW"]]$object,
  region = "Region1",
  cells_highlight = immune_types,
  pt.size.highlight = 1,
  pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
  stroke.size = 0.05,
  pal = pal,
  custom_theme = custom_theme
) + NoLegend()
print(p)
```

```{r Loop to save immune highlight plots for each object}
for (info_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[info_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  regions <- info$regions
  
  Idents(seurat_obj) <- "first_type"
  
  # Filter immune types present in object
  present_idents <- names(table(Idents(seurat_obj)))[table(Idents(seurat_obj)) > 0]
  valid_immune_types <- intersect(immune_types, present_idents)
  if (length(valid_immune_types) == 0) next
  
  for (region in regions) {
    # Generate the plot using the custom function
    p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
      seurat_obj = seurat_obj,
      region = region,
      cells_highlight = valid_immune_types,
      annotation_col = "first_type",
      pt.size.highlight = 1,
      pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
      stroke.size = 0.05,
      pal = pal,
      custom_theme = custom_theme
    ) +
      ggtitle(paste(info_name, region, "Immune Highlight")) +
      custom_theme +
      theme(
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
      ) +
      NoLegend()
    
    # Save plot
    ggsave_multidevice(
      filename_base = paste(info_name, region, "ImmuneHighlight", sep = "_"),
      plot = p,
      width = 7,
      height = 7
    )
  }
}

for (info_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[info_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  Idents(seurat_obj) <- "first_type"
  
  # Filter immune types present in object
  present_idents <- names(table(Idents(seurat_obj)))[table(Idents(seurat_obj)) > 0]
  valid_immune_types <- intersect(immune_types, present_idents)
  if (length(valid_immune_types) == 0) next
  
    # Generate the plot using the custom function
    p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
      seurat_obj = seurat_obj,
      region = ifelse(info_name %in% c("YWV01_UW","YWV02_D30PW"), "ROI","ROI1"),
      cells_highlight = valid_immune_types,
      annotation_col = "first_type",
      pt.size.highlight = 1.5,
      pt.size.unselected = 0,
      stroke.size = 0.05,
      pal = pal,
      custom_theme = custom_theme
    ) +
      ggtitle(paste(info_name, region, "Immune Highlight")) +
      custom_theme +
      theme(
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
      ) +
      NoLegend()
    
    # Save plot
    ggsave_multidevice(
      filename_base = paste(info_name, region, "ImmuneHighlight", sep = "_"),
      plot = p,
      width = 7,
      height = 7
    )
}
```
```{r Save cell type highlight plot with legend}
Idents(object_d7) <- "first_type"
immune_cells <- CellsByIdentities(object_d7, idents = immune_types)
immune_cells <- immune_cells[immune_types]
SpatialDimPlot(object_d7, images = "Region1", cells.highlight = immune_cells, 
               cols.highlight = pal) & custom_theme

ggsave("YWV03_D7PW_ImmuneHighlightLegend.png", width = 9, height = 6)
```

```{r Plot quantification of immune cells across objects}
plot_data <- bind_rows(
  get_metacluster_counts(object_uw, annotation_col = "first_type", "Unwounded")$region_counts,
  get_metacluster_counts(object_d4, annotation_col = "first_type", "D4PW")$region_counts,
  get_metacluster_counts(object_d7, annotation_col = "first_type", "D7PW")$region_counts,
  get_metacluster_counts(object_d30, annotation_col = "first_type", "D30PW")$region_counts
)

filtered_data <- plot_data %>%
  filter(first_type %in% immune_types)

filtered_data$Object <- factor(filtered_data$Object, levels = c("Unwounded","D4PW","D7PW","D30PW"))
filtered_data$first_type <- factor(filtered_data$first_type,
                                   levels = immune_types)

ggplot(filtered_data, aes(x = Object, y = Normalized, fill = first_type)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_jitter(
    aes(color = first_type),
    shape = 21,
    color = "black",
    position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.1, jitter.height = 0, dodge.width = 0.9),
    size = 2,
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "Proportion of Cell Types by Region Over Time",
    x = "Time Point",
    y = "Normalized Spot Count",
    fill = "Cell Type"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.x = element_blank()) + custom_theme

ggsave("ImmuneCells_VisiumHDPropBarplot.png", width = 12, height = 6)
```
```{r Define function to run stats on immune cells compared to unwounded}
# Statistical testing function
run_stats_vs_unwounded <- function(data, value_col) {
  # Value column is either "Normalized" or "Proportion"
  
  # Get the unwounded data
  unwounded_data <- data %>% 
    filter(Object == "Unwounded")
    
  # Get the data for D4PW, D7PW, D30PW
  wounded_timepoints <- c("D4PW", "D7PW", "D30PW")
  
  # Prepare an empty list to store results
  results_list <- list()
  
  for (time_point in wounded_timepoints) {
    # Data for the current time point
    time_point_data <- data %>% 
      filter(Object == time_point)
      
    # Iterate over each cell type
    for (cell_type in unique(data$first_type)) {
      
      # Filter data for the current cell type
      unwounded_subset <- unwounded_data %>% 
        filter(first_type == cell_type) %>% 
        pull(!!sym(value_col))
        
      time_point_subset <- time_point_data %>% 
        filter(first_type == cell_type) %>% 
        pull(!!sym(value_col))
        
      # Check if both groups have enough data to run the test
      if (length(unwounded_subset) > 1 & length(time_point_subset) > 1) {
        # Perform Wilcoxon rank-sum test
        test_result <- wilcox.test(time_point_subset, unwounded_subset, alternative = "two.sided")
        
        # Calculate the log2 fold change (D4PW/Unwounded)
        log2fc <- log2(mean(time_point_subset) / mean(unwounded_subset))
        
        # Store results
        results_list[[paste(time_point, cell_type, value_col, sep = "_")]] <- data.frame(
          Comparison = paste0(time_point, " vs Unwounded"),
          Cell_Type = cell_type,
          Metric = value_col,
          Log2FC = log2fc,
          P_value = test_result$p.value,
          W_statistic = test_result$statistic
        )
      } else {
        # Store results for cases with insufficient data
        results_list[[paste(time_point, cell_type, value_col, sep = "_")]] <- data.frame(
          Comparison = paste0(time_point, " vs Unwounded"),
          Cell_Type = cell_type,
          Metric = value_col,
          Log2FC = NA,
          P_value = NA,
          W_statistic = NA
        )
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Combine all results into a single data frame
  results_df <- bind_rows(results_list)
  
  # Apply multiple testing correction (e.g., Benjamini-Hochberg)
  results_df <- results_df %>%
    mutate(FDR = p.adjust(P_value, method = "BH")) %>%
    arrange(P_value)
    
  return(results_df)
}
```

```{r}
# --- R Code to Aggregate Total Immune Cell Counts ---

# Assuming 'counts_data' (from previous steps) contains all cell types, 
# and 'normalized_data' contains only immune subtypes.
# We will use 'normalized_data' as it already has the 'Normalized' count per region.

# 1. Aggregate the normalized counts across all immune subtypes (first_type) for each Region/Timepoint
total_immune_data <- normalized_data %>%
  # Group by the time point (Object) and the biological replicate (Region)
  group_by(Object, Region) %>%
  summarise(
    # Sum the Normalized counts (spots per 10k)
    Total_Normalized = sum(Normalized), 
    # Sum the Proportion counts (proportion of total spots)
    Total_Proportion = sum(Proportion), 
    .groups = "drop" # Drop the grouping
  ) %>%
  # Add a generic cell type label for plotting consistency
  mutate(Cell_Type = "Total Immune Cells")

# --- Plot A: Total Immune Normalized Count ---

# Calculate mean values for plotting the bar height
plot_means_norm <- total_immune_data %>%
  group_by(Object) %>%
  summarise(Mean = mean(Total_Normalized), .groups = 'drop')

total_norm_plot <- ggplot(total_immune_data, aes(x = Object, y = Total_Normalized)) +
  # Bar represents the mean value for the timepoint
  geom_col(
    data = plot_means_norm, 
    aes(x = Object, y = Mean), 
    fill = "skyblue", 
    color = "black",
    width = 0.7
  ) +
  # Jittered points represent the N=2 individual region values
  geom_jitter(
    width = 0.1, 
    shape = 21, 
    fill = "darkblue", 
    color = "black", 
    size = 3, 
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "Total Immune Cell Normalized Count Over Time",
    x = "Time Point",
    y = "Total Immune Spots (Normalized to 10k Spots)"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.x = element_blank()) + custom_theme
        
ggsave("TotalImmuneCells_NormalizedCount.png", plot = total_norm_plot, width = 6, height = 5)

# --- Plot B: Total Immune Proportion Out of All Spots ---

# Calculate mean values for plotting the bar height
plot_means_prop <- total_immune_data %>%
  group_by(Object) %>%
  summarise(Mean = mean(Total_Proportion), .groups = 'drop')

total_prop_plot <- ggplot(total_immune_data, aes(x = Object, y = Total_Proportion)) +
  # Bar represents the mean value for the timepoint
  geom_col(
    data = plot_means_prop, 
    aes(x = Object, y = Mean), 
    fill = "coral", 
    color = "black",
    width = 0.7
  ) +
  # Jittered points represent the N=2 individual region values
  geom_jitter(
    width = 0.1, 
    shape = 21, 
    fill = "darkred", 
    color = "black", 
    size = 3, 
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "Total Immune Cell Proportion of All Spots Over Time",
    x = "Time Point",
    y = "Total Immune Proportion (%)"
  ) +
  # Ensure the Y-axis starts at 0 for proportion plots
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.x = element_blank()) + custom_theme
        
ggsave("TotalImmuneCells_Proportion.png", plot = total_prop_plot, width = 6, height = 5)
```

```{r Quantify immune cells over time}
setwd("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Manuscripts/Wound Healing Atlas Paper/Immunome Paper/Figure 3 Spatial/Quantification")
# --- Load Libraries ---
library(Seurat)
library(tidyverse)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggsignif) 

# --- Define Quantification Functions (Unchanged) ---

# Function 1: Calculates metacluster counts/proportions relative to ALL cells
get_metacluster_counts <- function(seurat_obj, annotation_col, label, scaling_factor = 10000) {
  # Count raw metacluster values by Region
  count_data <- seurat_obj@meta.data %>%
    dplyr::count(Region, !!sym(annotation_col)) %>%
    group_by(Region) %>%
    mutate(
      Normalized = n / sum(n) * scaling_factor, # Normalized to total cells
      Proportion = n / sum(n) * 100             # Proportion relative to total cells (%)
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(Object = label)
    
  return(list(region_counts = count_data))
}

# Function 2: Calculates proportions relative to ALL IMMUNE cells
get_immune_proportions <- function(seurat_obj, annotation_col, immune_types, label) {
  
  # 1. Filter Seurat object metadata to include only immune cells
  immune_meta <- seurat_obj@meta.data %>%
    filter(!!sym(annotation_col) %in% immune_types)
  
  # 2. Count immune cell subtypes by Region
  immune_count_data <- immune_meta %>%
    dplyr::count(Region, !!sym(annotation_col)) %>%
    group_by(Region) %>%
    # Calculate proportion relative to the total number of immune cells in that Region
    mutate(
      Proportion_Immune = n / sum(n) * 100
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(Object = label)
    
  return(immune_count_data)
}

# --- Define Statistical Functions (Optimized for Wilcoxon Test) ---

# Helper function to convert p-values to significance stars
get_stars <- function(p) {
  stars <- case_when(
    p < 0.001 ~ "***",
    p < 0.01  ~ "**",
    p < 0.05  ~ "*",
    TRUE      ~ "ns"
  )
  return(stars)
}

# Function 3: Run stats for a specified metric (Normalized, Proportion, or Proportion_Immune) vs. Unwounded
# The Wilcoxon Rank-Sum test is appropriate for comparing independent group means/medians.
run_wilcox_test_vs_unwounded <- function(data, value_col) {
  
  unwounded_data <- data %>% filter(Object == "Unwounded")
  wounded_timepoints <- c("D4PW", "D7PW", "D30PW")
  results_list <- list()
  
  for (time_point in wounded_timepoints) {
    time_point_data <- data %>% filter(Object == time_point)
      
    for (cell_type in unique(data$first_type)) {
      
      unwounded_subset <- unwounded_data %>% 
        filter(first_type == cell_type) %>% pull(!!sym(value_col))
        
      time_point_subset <- time_point_data %>% 
        filter(first_type == cell_type) %>% pull(!!sym(value_col))
        
      if (length(unwounded_subset) > 1 & length(time_point_subset) > 1) {
        # Perform Wilcoxon Rank-Sum test (non-parametric comparison of medians/distributions)
        test_result <- wilcox.test(time_point_subset, unwounded_subset, alternative = "two.sided")
        log2fc <- log2(mean(time_point_subset) / mean(unwounded_subset))
        
        results_list[[paste(time_point, cell_type, value_col, sep = "_")]] <- data.frame(
          Comparison = paste0(time_point, " vs Unwounded"),
          Cell_Type = cell_type,
          Metric = value_col,
          Log2FC = log2fc,
          P_value = test_result$p.value
        )
      }
    }
  }
  
  results_df <- bind_rows(results_list)
  if (nrow(results_df) > 0) {
      results_df <- results_df %>%
        mutate(FDR = p.adjust(P_value, method = "BH")) %>%
        mutate(Stars = get_stars(FDR)) %>%
        arrange(P_value)
  }
  return(results_df)
}

# --- Plotting Function with Stacked/Dodged Logic and Stats Integration ---

# Function 4: Generates the plot and adds significance stars
generate_plot_with_stats <- function(plot_data, stats_data, value_col, y_label, title, filename, plot_type = "dodged") {
  
  # Calculate mean data for plotting stars' Y position
  plot_means <- plot_data %>%
    group_by(Object, first_type) %>%
    summarise(Mean_Value = mean(!!sym(value_col)), .groups = 'drop')
    
  # Prepare stats data for plotting (only significant comparisons)
  star_positions <- stats_data %>%
    filter(Stars != "ns") %>%
    separate(Comparison, into = c("End_Group", "Start_Group"), sep = " vs ", remove = FALSE) %>%
    # Join with mean values for Y position calculation
    left_join(plot_means, by = c("End_Group" = "Object", "Cell_Type" = "first_type")) %>%
    group_by(Cell_Type) %>%
    # Stagger Y positions for multiple stars on the same cell type
    mutate(
      # Use max value of the entire plot data for consistent staggering height
      max_plot_value = max(plot_data[[value_col]]),
      Label_Y = max_plot_value + 0.1 * max_plot_value + (row_number() * 0.05 * max_plot_value) 
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
    
  # Create the base plot
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Object, y = !!sym(value_col), fill = first_type)) +
    theme_bw() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = "Time Point",
      y = y_label,
      fill = "Cell Type"
    ) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.title.x = element_blank()) + custom_theme

  # Apply plot type logic
  if (plot_type == "dodged") {
      # Dodged bars for counts (easier to read individual type changes)
      p <- p +
        stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
        geom_jitter(
          shape = 21, color = "black", 
          position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.1, jitter.height = 0, dodge.width = 0.9),
          size = 2, alpha = 0.7
        )
      
      # Add stars for dodged plot (need to dodge them too)
      if (nrow(star_positions) > 0) {
        p <- p +
          geom_text(
            data = star_positions,
            aes(x = End_Group, y = Label_Y, label = Stars, group = Cell_Type),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
            size = 5,
            inherit.aes = FALSE 
          )
      }
      
  } else if (plot_type == "stacked") {
      # Stacked bars for proportions (to show 100% composition)
      p <- p +
        stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", position = "stack", color = "black") +
        # Note: Jittering raw data on a stacked bar plot can be misleading/difficult. 
        # We will use the bar plot only for composition visualization.
        # The stats stars apply to the overall proportions, which is the key comparison.
        
        # Add stars for stacked plot (no dodging needed)
        if (nrow(star_positions) > 0) {
          p <- p +
            geom_text(
              data = star_positions,
              aes(x = End_Group, y = Label_Y, label = Stars, group = Cell_Type),
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), # Using dodge(0.9) to prevent overlap on x-axis
              size = 5,
              inherit.aes = FALSE 
            )
        }
  }

  ggsave(filename, plot = p, width = 12, height = 6)
  return(p)
}

# --- EXECUTION: Data Processing & Analysis ---

# --- A. Prepare Data ---

# Normalized Data (relative to total cells)
counts_data <- bind_rows(
  get_metacluster_counts(object_uw, annotation_col = "first_type", "Unwounded")$region_counts,
  get_metacluster_counts(object_d4, annotation_col = "first_type", "D4PW")$region_counts,
  get_metacluster_counts(object_d7, annotation_col = "first_type", "D7PW")$region_counts,
  get_metacluster_counts(object_d30, annotation_col = "first_type", "D30PW")$region_counts
)
normalized_data <- counts_data %>% filter(first_type %in% immune_types)
normalized_data$Object <- factor(normalized_data$Object, levels = c("Unwounded","D4PW","D7PW","D30PW"))
normalized_data$first_type <- factor(normalized_data$first_type, levels = immune_types)

# Immune Proportion Data (relative to total immune cells)
immune_proportion_data <- bind_rows(
  get_immune_proportions(object_uw, annotation_col = "first_type", immune_types, "Unwounded"),
  get_immune_proportions(object_d4, annotation_col = "first_type", immune_types, "D4PW"),
  get_immune_proportions(object_d7, annotation_col = "first_type", immune_types, "D7PW"),
  get_immune_proportions(object_d30, annotation_col = "first_type", immune_types, "D30PW")
)
immune_proportion_data$Object <- factor(immune_proportion_data$Object, levels = c("Unwounded","D4PW","D7PW","D30PW"))
immune_proportion_data$first_type <- factor(immune_proportion_data$first_type, levels = immune_types)

# --- 1. Statistical Testing (Wilcoxon Rank-Sum Test) and CSV Saving ---

# 1.1. Stats for Normalized Counts
normalized_counts_stats <- run_wilcox_test_vs_unwounded(normalized_data, "Normalized")
write.csv(normalized_counts_stats, "ImmuneCell_NormalizedCounts_Stats_vs_Unwounded.csv", row.names = FALSE)
# [File: ImmuneCell_NormalizedCounts_Stats_vs_Unwounded.csv]

# 1.2. Stats for Immune Proportions
immune_proportions_stats <- run_wilcox_test_vs_unwounded(immune_proportion_data, "Proportion_Immune")
write.csv(immune_proportions_stats, "ImmuneCell_ImmuneProportion_Stats_vs_Unwounded.csv", row.names = FALSE)
# [File: ImmuneCell_ImmuneProportion_Stats_vs_Unwounded.csv]


# --- 2. Plot Generation with Stats Stars ---

# 2.1. Plot for Normalized Counts (DODGED)
normalized_counts_plot <- generate_plot_with_stats(
  plot_data = normalized_data,
  stats_data = normalized_counts_stats,
  value_col = "Normalized",
  y_label = "Normalized Spot Count (per 10,000 spots)",
  title = "Immune Cell Normalized Spot Counts Over Time",
  filename = "ImmuneCells_NormalizedCounts_Dodged_WithStats.png",
  plot_type = "dodged"
)

# 2.2. Plot for Immune Proportions (STACKED)
immune_proportions_plot <- generate_plot_with_stats(
  plot_data = immune_proportion_data,
  stats_data = immune_proportions_stats,
  value_col = "Proportion_Immune",
  y_label = "Proportion of Total Immune Cells (%)",
  title = "Immune Cell Subtype Composition (100% Immune)",
  filename = "ImmuneCell_ImmuneProportion_Stacked_WithStats.png",
  plot_type = "stacked"
)

cat("Analysis complete. Both plots generated (one dodged, one stacked).\n")
```
```{r Plot FC Relative to UW}
# --- Load Libraries ---
library(tidyverse)
library(RColorBrewer) 

# Assuming the following functions and objects are already defined and loaded from previous steps:
# - get_metacluster_counts, get_immune_proportions
# - run_wilcox_test_vs_unwounded (calculates Log2FC)
# - normalized_data, normalized_counts_stats (from previous execution)
# - immune_types, pal, custom_theme
# - The Seurat objects (object_uw, etc.)

# --- Dedicated Log2FC Plotting Function ---

plot_log2fc_relative_to_unwounded <- function(stats_data, title, filename, show_stats = TRUE) {
  
  # Ensure the data is filtered and ordered correctly
  plot_data <- stats_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(Log2FC)) %>%
    mutate(
      Cell_Type = factor(Cell_Type, levels = rev(unique(Cell_Type))), # Reverse order for horizontal plot
      Time_Point = gsub(" vs Unwounded", "", Comparison)
    )
    
  # Find the maximum absolute value for symmetric x-axis limits
  max_fc <- max(abs(plot_data$Log2FC)) * 1.1 
  
  log2fc_plot <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Log2FC, y = Cell_Type, fill = Time_Point)) +
    # Use geom_col for bar chart
    geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), color = "black", size = 0.3) +
    
    # Add a vertical line at Log2FC = 0 (no change)
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
    
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-max_fc, max_fc)) +
    
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = expression(log[2]~"Fold Change vs. Unwounded"),
      y = "Immune Cell Type",
      fill = "Time Point"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
      axis.title.y = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    ) + custom_theme
    
    # --- NEW LOGIC TO PLOT SIGNIFICANCE LABELS CONDITIONALLY ---
    if (show_stats) {
      log2fc_plot <- log2fc_plot +
        geom_text(
          aes(label = Stars),
          # Position the stars to the right of the bar, avoiding overlap with the bar itself
          # The sign(Log2FC) ensures the star is placed outward from the zero line
          hjust = ifelse(plot_data$Log2FC < 0, 1.1, -0.1), 
          position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
          size = 3.5,
          color = "black"
        )
    }
    # --- END NEW LOGIC ---
    
  ggsave(filename, plot = log2fc_plot, width = 10, height = 8)
  
  return(log2fc_plot)
}

# --- EXECUTION: Generate Log2FC Plot ---

# 1. Ensure normalized_counts_stats has been run (from previous code chunk)
# If running this independently, you must run the statistical analysis first:
# normalized_counts_stats <- run_wilcox_test_vs_unwounded(normalized_data, "Normalized")

# 2. Generate the plot
log2fc_plot <- plot_log2fc_relative_to_unwounded(
  stats_data = normalized_counts_stats,
  title = "Log2 Fold Change in Normalized Cell Counts Relative to Unwounded",
  filename = "ImmuneCells_NormalizedCounts_Log2FC_Plot.png",
  show_stats = FALSE
)

cat("Generated Log2FC plot: ImmuneCells_NormalizedCounts_Log2FC_Plot.png\n")
```

Save plots of specific immune subclusters
```{r Save plots of specific immune populations}
setwd("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Manuscripts/Wound Healing Atlas Paper/Immunome Paper/Figure 3 Spatial/Immune cell highlight plots")

immune_cells_highlight <- c("Macrophages I","Macrophages II","Macrophages IV","OC-like cells")

for (cond in names(plot_info)) {
  plot_celltypes(
    seurat_obj = plot_info[[cond]]$object,
    cells_highlight = immune_cells_highlight,
    annotation_col = "first_type",
    regions = plot_info[[cond]]$regions,
    pt.size.factor = plot_info[[cond]]$pt.size.factor,
    palette = pal
  )
}
```
Save plots of multiple cell types simultaneously
```{r Colabel immune cells - Mac I and III}
immune_types <- c("Macrophages I","Macrophages III")

# # Define palette for immune types
# pal_fun <- color("smoothrainbow")
# pal <- pal_fun(length(immune_types), range = c(0.1, 0.9))
# names(pal) <- immune_types
# pal <- c(pal, Unselected = "white")

# Loop through each condition/sample
for (info_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[info_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  regions <- info$regions
  pt.size <- info$pt.size.factor

  Idents(seurat_obj) <- "first_type"

  # Filter immune types to those actually present in the object
  present_idents <- names(table(Idents(seurat_obj)))[table(Idents(seurat_obj)) > 0]
  valid_immune_types <- intersect(immune_types, present_idents)

  # Skip if no immune types are present
  if (length(valid_immune_types) == 0) next

  # Identify immune cells
  immune_cells <- CellsByIdentities(seurat_obj, idents = valid_immune_types)

  # Skip if no cells were found
  if (length(immune_cells) == 0) next

  for (region in regions) {
   # Generate the plot using the custom function
    p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
      seurat_obj = seurat_obj,
      region = region,
      cells_highlight = valid_immune_types,
      annotation_col = "first_type",
      pt.size.highlight = 1.2,
      pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
      stroke.size = 0.05,
      pal = pal,
      custom_theme = custom_theme
    ) +
      custom_theme +
      theme(
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_blank()
      ) +
      NoLegend()
    
    # Save plot
    ggsave_multidevice(
      filename_base = paste(info_name, region, "MacI-III-Highlight", sep = "_"),
      plot = p,
      width = 7,
      height = 7
    )
  }
}
```
```{r Colabel immune cells - Mac II and IV}
immune_types <- c("Macrophages II","Macrophages IV")

# # Define palette for immune types
# pal_fun <- color("smoothrainbow")
# pal <- pal_fun(length(immune_types), range = c(0.1, 0.9))
# names(pal) <- immune_types
# pal <- c(pal, Unselected = "white")

# Loop through each condition/sample
for (info_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[info_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  regions <- info$regions
  pt.size <- info$pt.size.factor

  Idents(seurat_obj) <- "first_type"

  # Filter immune types to those actually present in the object
  present_idents <- names(table(Idents(seurat_obj)))[table(Idents(seurat_obj)) > 0]
  valid_immune_types <- intersect(immune_types, present_idents)

  # Skip if no immune types are present
  if (length(valid_immune_types) == 0) next

  # Identify immune cells
  immune_cells <- CellsByIdentities(seurat_obj, idents = valid_immune_types)

  # Skip if no cells were found
```
