```{r Colabel immune cells - Mac II and IV}
  if (length(immune_cells) == 0) next

  for (region in regions) {
   # Generate the plot using the custom function
    p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
      seurat_obj = seurat_obj,
      region = region,
      cells_highlight = valid_immune_types,
      annotation_col = "first_type",
      pt.size.highlight = 1,
      pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
      stroke.size = 0.05,
      pal = pal,
      custom_theme = custom_theme
    ) +
      custom_theme +
      theme(
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_blank()
      ) +
      NoLegend()
    
    # Save plot
    ggsave_multidevice(
      filename_base = paste(info_name, region, "MacII-IV-Highlight", sep = "_"),
      plot = p,
      width = 7,
      height = 7
    )
  }
}
```
```{r Save cell type highlight plot with legend}
Idents(object_d7) <- "first_type"
immune_cells <- CellsByIdentities(object_d7, idents = immune_types)
immune_cells <- immune_cells[immune_types]
SpatialDimPlot(object_d7, images = "Region1", cells.highlight = immune_cells, 
               cols.highlight = pal) & custom_theme

ggsave("YWV03_D7PW_MacHighlightLegend.png", width = 9, height = 6)
```
```{r Define a function to plot individual cell types for a seurat object}
# Define function, using custom plotting function from above, to save plots
plot_celltypes <- function(seurat_obj, 
                           cells_highlight,
                           annotation_col,
                           regions, 
                           pt.size.factor = 1, 
                           palette, 
                           stroke.size = 0.05,
                           plot_height = 7,
                           plot_width = 7) {
  # Set identities to cell type annotation
  Idents(seurat_obj) <- annotation_col
  
  for (region in regions) {
    # Loop through each cell type in cells_highlight
    for (cell_type in cells_highlight) {

      # Generate plot with the custom function
      p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
        seurat_obj = seurat_obj,
        region = region,
        annotation_col = annotation_col,
        cells_highlight = cell_type,  # highlight one cell type at a time
        pt.size.highlight = pt.size.factor * 1,
        pt.size.unselected = pt.size.factor * 0.25,
        stroke.size = stroke.size,
        pal = palette,
        custom_theme = custom_theme
      ) +
        custom_theme +
        theme(panel.grid = element_blank()) +
        NoLegend()
      
      # Save plot
      ggsave_multidevice(
        filename_base = paste(unique(seurat_obj$orig.ident), region, cell_type, "HighlightPlot", sep = "_"),
        plot = p,
        width = plot_width,
        height = plot_height
      )
    }
  }
}
```
Save plots of each cell type individually 
```{r Save individual highlights of macrophages I-IV}
mac_types <- c("Macrophages I","Macrophages II","Macrophages III","Macrophages IV")

setwd("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Sequencing Repository/Users/Simon/Spatial Data/Immune Cell highlight plots")

for (cond in names(plot_info)) {
  plot_celltypes(
    seurat_obj = plot_info[[cond]]$object,
    cells_highlight = mac_types,
    annotation_col = "first_type",
    regions = plot_info[[cond]]$regions,
    pt.size.factor = plot_info[[cond]]$pt.size.factor,
    palette = pal
  )
}
```
```{r Save plot of all macrophage populations}
setwd("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Sequencing Repository/Users/Simon/Spatial Data/Immune Cell highlight plots")
for (info_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[info_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  regions <- info$regions
  
  Idents(seurat_obj) <- "first_type"
  
  for (region in regions) {
    # Generate the plot using the custom function
    p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
      seurat_obj = seurat_obj,
      region = region,
      cells_highlight = mac_types,
      annotation_col = "first_type",
      pt.size.highlight = 1,
      pt.size.unselected = 0.25,
      stroke.size = 0.05,
      pal = pal,
      custom_theme = custom_theme
    ) +
      theme(
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
      ) +
      NoLegend()
    
    # Save plot
    ggsave_multidevice(
      filename_base = paste(info_name, region, "MacrophageHighlight", sep = "_"),
      plot = p,
      width = 7,
      height = 7
    )
  }
}

```

Quantify immune cell representation across wound sites in the data
```{r Quantify immune representation across wound sites}
# Initialize an empty list to store results
all_results <- list()

for (cond in names(plot_info)) {
  obj <- plot_info[[cond]]$object
  
  # Quantify counts per region for immune cells
  results_df <- obj@meta.data %>%
    filter(first_type %in% immune_types) %>%
    count(Region, WoundSite, first_type, name = "Count") %>%
    mutate(Condition = cond)  # add condition info
  
  # Compute mean counts, SEM, and proportions
  mean_df <- results_df %>%
    group_by(WoundSite, first_type, Condition) %>%
    summarise(
      MeanCount = mean(Count, na.rm = TRUE),
      SEM       = sd(Count, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),   # standard error
      TotalCount = sum(Count, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    group_by(WoundSite, Condition) %>%
    mutate(
      Prop = TotalCount / sum(TotalCount)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  all_results[[cond]] <- mean_df
}

# Combine into one dataframe
immune_quant_df <- bind_rows(all_results)

# Set levels for plotting
## Timepoint
immune_quant_df$Condition <- factor(immune_quant_df$Condition, 
                                    levels = c("YWV01_UW","YWV04_D4PW","YWV03_D7PW","YWV02_D30PW"))
## Wound site
immune_quant_df$WoundSite <- factor(immune_quant_df$WoundSite, 
                                    levels = c("Periphery","WoundEdge","Wound"))

for(site in unique(immune_quant_df$WoundSite)){
    # Filter to wound site of interest
    periphery_df <- immune_quant_df %>%
      filter(WoundSite == site)
    
    # --- Counts over time ---
    ggplot(periphery_df, aes(x = Condition, y = MeanCount, fill = first_type)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", color = "grey25") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
      labs(title = paste0("Immune Cell Counts in ",site," Over Time"),
           y = "Cell Count",
           fill = "Cell Type") +
      ggprism::theme_prism() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(),
            axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
            axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) + 
      custom_theme + NoLegend()
    ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0("ImmuneCounts_",site,"_BarPlot"), width = 10, height = 6)
    
    # --- Proportions over time ---
    ggplot(periphery_df, aes(x = Condition, y = Prop, fill = first_type)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
      scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
      labs(title =  paste0("Immune Cell Proportions in ",site," Over Time"),
           x = "Condition",
           y = "Proportion of Immune Cells",
           fill = "Cell Type") +
      ggprism::theme_prism() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(),
            axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
            axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) + 
      custom_theme + NoLegend()
    ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0("ImmuneProps_",site,"_BarPlot"), width = 10, height = 6)
}

for(selected_timepoint in names(plot_info)){

    timepoint_df <- immune_quant_df %>%
      filter(Condition == selected_timepoint)
    
    # --- Counts across wound sites ---
    ggplot(timepoint_df, aes(x = WoundSite, y = MeanCount, fill = first_type)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", color = "grey25") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
      labs(title = paste("Immune Cell Counts -", selected_timepoint),
           x = "Wound Site",
           y = "Cell Count",
           fill = "Cell Type") +
      ggprism::theme_prism() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(),
            axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
            axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) + 
      custom_theme + NoLegend()
    ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0("ImmuneWoundSiteCounts_",selected_timepoint,"_BarPlot"), width = 10, height = 6)
    
    # --- Proportions across wound sites ---
    ggplot(timepoint_df, aes(x = WoundSite, y = Prop, fill = first_type)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
      scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
      labs(title = paste("Immune Cell Proportions -", selected_timepoint),
           x = "Wound Site",
           y = "Proportion of Immune Cells",
           fill = "Cell Type") +
      ggprism::theme_prism() + 
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(),
            axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
            axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) + 
      custom_theme + NoLegend()
    ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0("ImmuneWoundSiteProps_",selected_timepoint,"_BarPlot"), width = 10, height = 6)
}
```
Also save a plot for just unwounded which is formatted to match the scRNAseq
```{r Save UW plot with matched formatting to scRNAseq}
timepoint_df <- immune_quant_df %>%
      filter(Condition == "YWV01_UW")
    
    # --- Counts across wound sites ---
    ggplot(timepoint_df, aes(x = first_type, y = MeanCount, fill = first_type)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", width = 0.6) +
      geom_errorbar(
        aes(ymin = MeanCount - SEM, ymax = MeanCount + SEM),
        width = 0.2,
        position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)
        ) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
      labs(title = paste("Unwounded Immune Cell Counts from ST"),
           x = "Wound Site",
           y = "Cell Count",
           fill = "Cell Type") +
      ggprism::theme_prism(axis_text_angle = 45) +
      theme(
            axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
            axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),  # Remove major grid lines
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) + 
      custom_theme + NoLegend()
    
ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0("YWV01_UW_ImmuneCounts_BarPlot"), width = 9, height = 6)
```
Lastly, in conjunction with the immune analysis, we can examine the localization
of various populations associated with the three modes of immune recruitment
First, save vectors of immune cells for each dynamic
```{r Save immune cell lists by recruitment mode}
recruitment <- c("Monocytes I","Monocytes II","Monocytes III","Macrophages I","Macrophages III",
                 "OC-like cells","moDCs","Neutrophils I","Neutrophils II","Basophils")

restoration <-c("cDC1","cDC2","pDCs","Neutrophils III","Mast cells","NK cells","T cells","B cells")

neostasis <- c("Macrophages II","Macrophages IV","Cycling Macrophages",
               "Migratory DCs","Langerhans cells","γδT cells","Tregs","ILCs")

# Save each vector to a list
recruitment_modes <- list("Recruitment" = recruitment,
                          "Restoration" = restoration,
                          "Neostasis" = neostasis)
```

```{r Save plots of immune cell types grouped by temporal dynamics}
# Iterate over the list of modes to save plots
for(mode in names(recruitment_modes)){
    # Create a new folder for output files
    new.dir <- paste0(getwd(),"/",mode," Highlight plots")
    dir.create(new.dir)
    setwd(new.dir)
    
    for (info_name in names(plot_info)) {
      info <- plot_info[[info_name]]
      seurat_obj <- info$object
      regions <- info$regions
      
      Idents(seurat_obj) <- "first_type"
      
      for (region in regions) {
        # Generate the plot using the custom function
        p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
          seurat_obj = seurat_obj,
          region = region,
          cells_highlight = recruitment_modes[[mode]],
          annotation_col = "first_type",
          pt.size.highlight = 1,
          pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
          stroke.size = 0.05,
          pal = pal,
          custom_theme = custom_theme
        ) +
          theme(
            panel.grid = element_blank(),
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
          ) +
          NoLegend()
        
        # Save plot
        ggsave_multidevice(
          filename_base = paste(info_name, region, mode, "Highlight", sep = "_"),
          plot = p,
          width = 7,
          height = 7
        )
      }
      rm(seurat_obj)
    }
    
    # Save one plot with the legend
    p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
      seurat_obj = plot_info[["YWV04_D4PW"]]$object,
      region = "Region1",
      cells_highlight = recruitment_modes[[mode]],
      annotation_col = "first_type",
      pt.size.highlight = 1.25,
      pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
      stroke.size = 0.05,
      pal = pal,
      custom_theme = custom_theme
    ) 
    ggsave(paste0("YWV04_D4PW_",mode,"_Legend.png"), plot = p)
}
```

```{r Save immune modes with single color}
# Phase palette you provided
phase.pal <- c(
  "Recruitment" = "#F8766D",
  "Restoration" = "#00BA38",
  "Neostasis"   = "#619CFF"
)

# Loop over all modes
for (mode in names(recruitment_modes)) {

  # Make output folder
  out.dir <- file.path(getwd(), paste0(mode, " Highlight plots"))
  dir.create(out.dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

  # Build color mapping: every highlighted cell type gets SAME color
  highlight_colors <- setNames(
    rep(phase.pal[mode], length(recruitment_modes[[mode]])),
    recruitment_modes[[mode]]
  )

  # Loop over datasets
  for (info_name in names(plot_info)) {

    info <- plot_info[[info_name]]
    seurat_obj <- info$object
    regions <- info$regions

    Idents(seurat_obj) <- "first_type"

    # Loop over regions
    for (region in regions) {

      p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
        seurat_obj = seurat_obj,
        region = region,
        cells_highlight = recruitment_modes[[mode]],
        annotation_col = "first_type",
        pt.size.highlight = 1,
        pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
        stroke.size = 0.05,
        pal = c(highlight_colors, Unselected = "white"),    
        custom_theme = custom_theme
      ) +
        theme(
          panel.grid = element_blank(),
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
        ) +
        NoLegend()

      # Save per-region plot without legend
      ggsave_multidevice(
        filename_base = paste(info_name, region, mode, "Highlight", sep = "_"),
        plot = p,
        width = 7,
        height = 7
      )
    }

    rm(seurat_obj)
  }

  # One plot WITH legend (fixed dataset + Region1)
  legend_plot <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
    seurat_obj = plot_info[["YWV04_D4PW"]]$object,
    region = "Region1",
    cells_highlight = recruitment_modes[[mode]],
    annotation_col = "first_type",
    pt.size.highlight = 1.25,
    pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
    stroke.size = 0.05,
    pal =  c(highlight_colors, Unselected = "white"),
    custom_theme = custom_theme
  )

  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(out.dir, paste0("YWV04_D4PW_", mode, "_Legend.png")),
    plot = legend_plot,
    width = 7,
    height = 7
  )
}

```

```{r Save highlight plots of all immune modes colored by mode}
# Phase palette you provided
# Combine all highlighted cell types
all_highlight <- unlist(recruitment_modes)

# Build per-cell color map
highlight_colors <- unlist(lapply(names(recruitment_modes), function(mode){
  setNames(
    rep(phase.pal[mode], length(recruitment_modes[[mode]])),
    recruitment_modes[[mode]]
  )
}))

# Save list of images
dataset_regions <- list(
  YWV01_UW    = c("uw_Region1",  "uw_Region2"),
  YWV02_D30PW = c("d30_Region1", "d30_Region2"),
  YWV03_D7PW  = c("d7_Region1",  "d7_Region2"),
  YWV04_D4PW  = c("d4_Region1",  "d4_Region2")
)

# Loop to save combined-mode plots
for(dataset in names(dataset_regions)){

  regions <- dataset_regions[[dataset]]

  for(region in regions){

    p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
      seurat_obj = merged_st,
      dataset = dataset,
      region = region,
      annotation_col = "first_type",
      cells_highlight = all_highlight,
      pal = c(highlight_colors, Unselected = "white"),
      stroke.size = 0.01,
      pt.size.highlight = 1.5,
      pt.size.unselected = 0.25,
      custom_theme = custom_theme,
      show_scalebar = F
    ) +
      NoLegend()

    ggsave_multidevice(
      filename_base = paste0("ST_", dataset, "_", region, "_TemporalModes"),
      plot = p,
      height = 8,
      width = 8
    )
  }
}

```

With the groupings, we can then ask where these populations primarily localize in
relation to wound zones
```{r Quantify cell representation by temporal dynamics and wound zone}
# Build lookup table from recruitment_modes list
mode_lookup <- enframe(recruitment_modes, name = "Mode", value = "CellTypes") %>%
  unnest(CellTypes) %>%
  rename("CellTypes" = "first_type")

# Initialize an empty list to store results
all_results <- list()

for (cond in names(plot_info)) {
  obj <- plot_info[[cond]]$object

  # Quantify counts per region for immune cells
  results_df <- obj@meta.data %>%
    filter(first_type %in% immune_types) %>%
    count(Region, WoundSite, first_type, name = "Count") %>%
    mutate(Condition = cond)

  # Compute mean + SEM across Regions
  mean_df <- results_df %>%
    group_by(Condition, WoundSite, first_type) %>%
    summarise(
      MeanCount = mean(Count, na.rm = TRUE),
      SEM       = sd(Count, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
      .groups   = "drop"
    )

  all_results[[cond]] <- mean_df
}

# Combine into one dataframe
immune_quant_df <- bind_rows(all_results)

# Add mode annotations
immune_quant_modes <- immune_quant_df %>%
  left_join(mode_lookup, by = "first_type") %>%
  filter(!is.na(Mode))

# Collapse to Mode level: sum of mean counts across cell types in that Mode
mode_summary <- immune_quant_modes %>%
  group_by(Condition, WoundSite, Mode) %>%
  summarise(
    MeanCount = sum(MeanCount, na.rm = TRUE),
    SEM       = sqrt(sum(SEM^2, na.rm = TRUE)),  # combine SEMs conservatively
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Normalize to proportions within each site if desired
mode_summary <- mode_summary %>%
  group_by(Condition, WoundSite) %>%
  mutate(Prop = MeanCount / sum(MeanCount)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Set factor levels for plotting
mode_summary$Condition <- factor(
  mode_summary$Condition,
  levels = c("YWV01_UW","YWV04_D4PW","YWV03_D7PW","YWV02_D30PW")
)
mode_summary$Mode <- factor(
  mode_summary$Mode,
  levels = c("Recruitment","Restoration","Neostasis")
)
mode_summary$WoundSite <- factor(
  mode_summary$WoundSite,
  levels = c("Wound","WoundEdge","Periphery")
)

# --- Plot using mean counts (bars or alluvial) ---
ggplot(mode_summary,
  aes(x = Condition, stratum = Mode, alluvium = Mode, y = MeanCount, fill = Mode)) +
  ggalluvial::geom_flow(stat = "alluvium") +
  ggalluvial::geom_stratum() +
  facet_wrap(~WoundSite) +
  ggprism::theme_prism() +
  labs(x = "Timepoint",
       y = "Mean count across regions")

ggplot(mode_summary,
  aes(x = Condition, stratum = Mode, alluvium = Mode, y = Prop, fill = Mode)) +
  ggalluvial::geom_flow(stat = "alluvium") +
  ggalluvial::geom_stratum(width = 2/3) +
  facet_wrap(~WoundSite) +
  ggprism::theme_prism() +
  labs(x = "Timepoint",
       y = "Proportion")
ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = "ST_ImmuneModes_WoundSitePropBarplot", width = 8, height = 4)

ggplot(mode_summary, aes(x = as.numeric(factor(Condition)), y = Prop, color = Mode)) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, method = "loess") +
  facet_wrap(~WoundSite) +
  ggprism::theme_prism() +
  labs(x = "Timepoint",
       y = "Proportion") +
  custom_theme
ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = "ST_ImmuneModes_WoundSitePropLinegraph", width = 8, height = 4)
```
### Emergent cell types
Now we can highlight cell types that we identified in OWHA and wish to validate
```{r Define cell types of interest}
human_celltypes <- c("Outer Bulge III","Myofibroblasts IV", "Lox+ Fibroblasts", 
               "Vein ECs")

major_celltypes <- c("Reticular III","Papillary II","Myofibroblasts II","Basal IV","Spinous I","HF I")

minor_celltypes <- c("Fascia","Proliferating ECs","Muscle Progenitors","Repair SC II","Pericyte I","Adipocyte II")

cells_to_plot <- c("Spinous I","Proliferating ECs","Pericyte I","HF I")


cells_to_plot <- c("Basal IV")
```
We can quantify the proportions of cell types of interest across multiple objects
```{r Quantify specific cell type representation}
plot_data <- bind_rows(
  get_metacluster_counts(object_uw, annotation_col = "first_type", "Unwounded")$region_counts,
  get_metacluster_counts(object_d4, annotation_col = "first_type", "D4PW")$region_counts,
  get_metacluster_counts(object_d7, annotation_col = "first_type", "D7PW")$region_counts,
  get_metacluster_counts(object_d30, annotation_col = "first_type", "D30PW")$region_counts
)

filtered_data <- plot_data %>%
  filter(first_type %in% human_celltypes)

filtered_data$Object <- factor(filtered_data$Object, levels = c("Unwounded","D4PW","D7PW","D30PW"))
filtered_data$first_type <- factor(filtered_data$first_type,
                                   levels = c("Lox+ Fibroblasts", "Myofibroblasts IV","Outer Bulge III", "Vein ECs"))

ggplot(filtered_data, aes(x = Object, y = Proportion, fill = first_type)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_jitter(
    aes(color = first_type),
    shape = 21,
    color = "black",
    position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.1, jitter.height = 0, dodge.width = 0.9),
    size = 2,
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cell_colors) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "Proportion of Cell Types by Region Over Time",
    x = "Time Point",
    y = "Proportion of Spots (%)",
    fill = "Cell Type"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.x = element_blank()) + custom_theme

ggsave("HumanCellTypes_VisiumHDPropBarplot.png", width = 9, height = 6)
```
Save plots of emergent cell types using the custom highlighting function
```{r Save plots of major cell types}
# Add color for unselected cells to palette
cell_colors <- c(cell_colors, Unselected = "white")

for (info_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[info_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  regions <- info$regions
  
  Idents(seurat_obj) <- "first_type"
  
  for (region in regions) {
    # Generate the plot using the custom function
    p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
      seurat_obj = seurat_obj,
      region = region,
      cells_highlight = major_celltypes,
      annotation_col = "first_type",
      pt.size.highlight = 1,
      pt.size.unselected = 0.25,
      stroke.size = 0.05,
      pal = cell_colors,
      custom_theme = custom_theme
    ) +
      theme(
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
      ) +
      NoLegend()
    
    # Save plot
    ggsave_multidevice(
      filename_base = paste(info_name, region, "MajorCelltypesHighlight", sep = "_"),
      plot = p,
      width = 7,
      height = 7
    )
  }
}

# Save one plot with the legend
p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
  seurat_obj = plot_info[["YWV04_D4PW"]]$object,
  region = "Region1",
  cells_highlight = major_celltypes,
  annotation_col = "first_type",
  pt.size.highlight = 1.25,
  pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
  stroke.size = 0.05,
  pal = cell_colors,
  custom_theme = custom_theme
) 
ggsave_multidevice("YWV04_D4PW_MajorCellTypes_Legend", plot = p)
```

```{r Save plots of minor cell types}
# Add color for unselected cells to palette
cell_colors <- c(cell_colors, Unselected = "white")

for (info_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[info_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  regions <- info$regions
  
  Idents(seurat_obj) <- "first_type"
  
  for (region in regions) {
    # Generate the plot using the custom function
    p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
      seurat_obj = seurat_obj,
      region = region,
      cells_highlight = minor_celltypes,
      annotation_col = "first_type",
      pt.size.highlight = 1,
      pt.size.unselected = 0.25,
      stroke.size = 0.05,
      pal = cell_colors,
      custom_theme = custom_theme
    ) +
      theme(
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
      ) +
      NoLegend()
    
    # Save plot
    ggsave_multidevice(
      filename_base = paste(info_name, region, "MinorCelltypesHighlight", sep = "_"),
      plot = p,
      width = 7,
      height = 7
    )
  }
}

# Save one plot with the legend
p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
  seurat_obj = plot_info[["YWV04_D4PW"]]$object,
  region = "Region1",
  cells_highlight = minor_celltypes,
  annotation_col = "first_type",
  pt.size.highlight = 1.25,
  pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
  stroke.size = 0.05,
  pal = cell_colors,
  custom_theme = custom_theme
) 
ggsave_multidevice("YWV04_D4PW_MinorCellTypes_Legend", plot = p)
```
We can also combine the lists and save them together
```{r Save major and minor celltypes in one plot}
# Add color for unselected cells to palette
cell_colors <- c(cell_colors, Unselected = "white")

for (info_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[info_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  regions <- info$regions
  
  Idents(seurat_obj) <- "first_type"
  
  for (region in regions) {
    # Generate the plot using the custom function
    p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
      seurat_obj = seurat_obj,
      region = region,
      cells_highlight = c(major_celltypes, minor_celltypes),
      annotation_col = "first_type",
      pt.size.highlight = 1,
      pt.size.unselected = 0.25,
      stroke.size = 0.05,
      pal = cell_colors,
      custom_theme = custom_theme
    ) +
      theme(
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
      ) +
      NoLegend()
    
    # Save plot
    ggsave_multidevice(
      filename_base = paste(info_name, region, "MajorMinorCelltypesHighlight", sep = "_"),
      plot = p,
      width = 7,
      height = 7
    )
  }
}

# Save one plot with the legend
p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
  seurat_obj = plot_info[["YWV04_D4PW"]]$object,
  region = "Region1",
  cells_highlight = c(major_celltypes, minor_celltypes),
  annotation_col = "first_type",
  pt.size.highlight = 1.25,
  pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
  stroke.size = 0.05,
  pal = cell_colors,
  custom_theme = custom_theme
) 
ggsave_multidevice("YWV04_D4PW_MajorMinorCellTypes_Legend", plot = p)
```

We can also supply each of our cell type lists to save plots for each.
```{r Iterate over objects to save individual highlight plots of major and minor cell types}
# Major cell types
setwd("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Manuscripts/Wound Healing Atlas Paper/Hair Follicle Paper/Shared Data/Spatial Data/Figures/Major Cell Types")
for (cond in names(plot_info)) {
  plot_celltypes(
    seurat_obj = plot_info[[cond]]$object,
    cells_highlight = major_celltypes,
    annotation_col = "first_type",
    regions = plot_info[[cond]]$regions,
    pt.size.factor = plot_info[[cond]]$pt.size.factor,
  palette = cell_colors
  )
}
# Minor cell types
setwd("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Manuscripts/Wound Healing Atlas Paper/Hair Follicle Paper/Shared Data/Spatial Data/Figures/Minor Cell Types")
for (cond in names(plot_info)) {
  plot_celltypes(
    seurat_obj = plot_info[[cond]]$object,
    cells_highlight = minor_celltypes,
    annotation_col = "first_type",
    regions = plot_info[[cond]]$regions,
    pt.size.factor = plot_info[[cond]]$pt.size.factor,
  palette = cell_colors
  )
}
```
```{r Save D7PW WoundEdge ROI plots for specific cell types}
plot_celltypes(
    seurat_obj =object_d7,
    cells_highlight = cells_to_plot,
    annotation_col = "first_type",
    regions = "WoundEdgeROI",
    pt.size.factor = 6,
    stroke.size = 0.1,
    palette = cell_colors,
    plot_height = 7.5,
    plot_width = 7
  )

p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
  seurat_obj = object_d7,
  region = "WoundEdgeROI",
  cells_highlight =  c(major_celltypes, minor_celltypes),
  annotation_col = "first_type",
  pt.size.highlight = 6,
  pt.size.unselected = 1.5,
  stroke.size = 0.1,
  pal = cell_colors,
  custom_theme = custom_theme
) 
ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = "YWV03_D7PW_MajorMinorCellTypes_WoundEdgeROI_Legend", plot = p)

p + NoLegend()
ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = "YWV03_D7PW_MajorMinorCellTypes_WoundEdgeROI", height = 7.5)

cluster_pal <- setNames(viridis(length(unique(object_d7$banksy_cluster_0.5)), option = "H"),levels(object_d7$banksy_cluster_0.5))

p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
  seurat_obj = object_d7,
  region = "WoundEdgeROI",
  cells_highlight =  unique(object_d7$banksy_cluster_0.5),
  annotation_col = "banksy_cluster_0.5",
  pt.size.highlight = 2.5,
  pt.size.unselected = 1.5,
  stroke.size = 0.05,
  pal = cluster_pal,
  custom_theme = custom_theme
) + NoLegend()
p

ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = "YWV03_D7PW_BanksyClusters_WoundEdgeROI", height = 7.5)

SpatialDimPlot(
  object = object_d7,
  images = "WoundEdgeROI",
  group.by = "banksy_cluster_0.5",
  pt.size.factor = 6,
  cols = cluster_pal
) & custom_theme + NoLegend()
```

```{r}
source("~/Documents/Shared-Utils/Useful Functions/CustomSpatialHighlightPlot2.R")
```

```{r Save colabel plots of fascia and macrophages}
setwd("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Sequencing Repository/Users/Simon/Spatial Data/Immune Cell highlight plots/Macrophage Fascia Colabeling")

cols <- c(cell_colors["Fascia"], pal)

cells_to_plot <- c("Fascia","Macrophages I","Macrophages II","Macrophages III",
                   "Macrophages IV")

for (info_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[info_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  regions <- info$regions
  dataset <- unique(seurat_obj$orig.ident)
  
  Idents(seurat_obj) <- "first_type"
  
  for (region in regions) {
    # Generate the plot using the custom function
    p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
      seurat_obj = seurat_obj,
      dataset = dataset,
      region = region,
      cells_highlight = cells_to_plot,
      annotation_col = "first_type",
      pt.size.highlight = 1,
      pt.size.unselected = 0.25,
      stroke.size = 0.05,
      pal = cols,
      custom_theme = custom_theme
    ) +
      theme(
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
      ) +
      NoLegend()
    
    # Save plot
    ggsave_multidevice(
      filename_base = paste(info_name, region, "MacFasciaHighlight", sep = "_"),
      plot = p,
      width = 7,
      height = 7
    )
  }
}

# # Save one plot with the legend
# p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
#   seurat_obj = plot_info[["YWV04_D4PW"]]$object,
#   region = "Region1",
#   cells_highlight = major_celltypes,
#   annotation_col = "first_type",
#   pt.size.highlight = 1.25,
#   pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
#   stroke.size = 0.05,
#   pal = cell_colors,
#   custom_theme = custom_theme
# ) 
# ggsave_multidevice("YWV04_D4PW_MajorCellTypes_Legend", plot = p)
```

We can also use the function to save individual plots of metaclusters for each object
```{r Save individual plots of each metacluster}
setwd("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Manuscripts/Wound Healing Atlas Paper/Hair Follicle Paper/Shared Data/Spatial Data/Figures/Cell Type Annotation/Individual Metacluster highlight plots")

for (cond in names(plot_info)) {
  plot_celltypes(
    seurat_obj = plot_info[[cond]]$object,
    cells_highlight = names(metacluster_colors),
    annotation_col = "metaclusters",
    regions = plot_info[[cond]]$regions,
    pt.size.factor = plot_info[[cond]]$pt.size.factor,
    palette = metacluster_colors
  )
}
```

```{r Load custom feature plot for coexpression}
source("~/Documents/Shared-Utils/Useful Functions/CustomSpatialFeatureCoexpPlot.R")
```

```{r Use custom feature plot to plot multiple features at once for OC-like inducing genes}
feats <- c("Tnfsf11","Tnfrsf11a","Tnfrsf11b","Mmp3")
feat_colors <- setNames(c("#D53E4F","#3288BD","#66C2A5","#FDAE61"),feats) 

p_uw <- CustomSpatialFeatureCoexpPlot(
  object = object_uw,
  image = "Region2",
  genes = feats,
  gene_colors = feat_colors,
  assay = "Spatial",
  pt.size.expr = 0.75,
  pt.size.zero = 0.25,
  bg.col       = "grey95"
) & custom_theme 
p_uw

p_d4 <- CustomSpatialFeatureCoexpPlot(
  object = object_d4,
  image = "Region1",
  genes = feats,
  gene_colors = feat_colors,
  assay = "Spatial",
  pt.size.expr = 0.75,
  pt.size.zero = 0.25,
  bg.col       = "grey95"
) & custom_theme 
p_d4

p_d7 <- CustomSpatialFeatureCoexpPlot(
  object = object_d7,
  image = "Region1",
  genes = feats,
  gene_colors = feat_colors,
  assay = "Spatial",
  pt.size.expr = 0.75,
  pt.size.zero = 0.25,
  bg.col       = "grey95"
) & custom_theme 
p_d7

p_d30 <- CustomSpatialFeatureCoexpPlot(
  object = object_d30,
  image = "Region1",
  genes = feats,
  gene_colors = feat_colors,
  assay = "Spatial",
  pt.size.expr = 0.75,
  pt.size.zero = 0.25,
  bg.col       = "grey95"
)
p_d30

ggsave("YWV01_UW_OClikeInducersFeatPlot.png", plot = p_uw, width = 7, height = 8)
ggsave("YWV02_D30PW_OClikeInducersFeatPlot.png", plot = p_d30, width = 7, height = 8)
ggsave("YWV04_D4PW_OClikeInducersFeatPlot.png", plot = p_d4, width = 7, height = 8)
ggsave("YWV03_D7PW_OClikeInducersFeatPlot.png", plot = p_d7, width = 7, height = 8)
```
We can then loop through the objects to save more plots, this time of a region of
interest
```{r Loop to save OC-inducing genes for ROI}
for(cond in names(plot_info)){
  p <- CustomSpatialFeatureCoexpPlot(
    object = plot_info[[cond]]$object,
    image = ifelse(cond %in% c("YWV01_UW","YWV02_D30PW"),"ROI","ROI1"),
    genes = feats,
    gene_colors = feat_colors,
    assay = "Spatial",
    pt.size.expr = 1.5,
    pt.size.zero = 0,
    bg.col       = "grey99"
  ) & custom_theme 
  ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(cond,"_OCInducerFeatplot_ROI"),
                     width = 7, height = 8)
}

# We can also loop over the features to save each one individually
for(cond in names(plot_info)){
  for(feat in feats){
  p <- CustomSpatialFeatureCoexpPlot(
    object = plot_info[[cond]]$object,
    image = ifelse(cond %in% c("YWV01_UW","YWV02_D30PW"),"ROI","ROI1"),
    genes = feat,
    gene_colors = feat_colors[feat],
    assay = "Spatial",
    pt.size.expr = 1.5,
    pt.size.zero = 0.5,
    bg.col       = "grey99"
  ) & custom_theme 
  ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(cond,"_",feat,"Featplot_ROI"),
                     width = 7, height = 8)
  }
}
```

```{r Plot expression of OC-inducing genes on dotplot}
feats <- c("Tnfsf11","Tnfrsf11a","Tnfrsf11b","Mmp3")

DotPlot(object_uw, feats, assay = "Spatial", group.by = "first_type") + 
  theme(axis.title = element_blank()) + custom_theme
ggsave("YWV01_UW_OCInducerDotPlot.png", width = 8,height = 18)

DotPlot(object_d4, feats, assay = "Spatial", group.by = "first_type")+ 
  theme(axis.title = element_blank()) + custom_theme
ggsave("YWV04_D4PW_OCInducerDotPlot.png", width = 8,height = 18)

DotPlot(object_d7, feats, assay = "Spatial", group.by = "first_type")+ 
  theme(axis.title = element_blank()) + custom_theme
ggsave("YWV03_D7PW_OCInducerDotPlot.png", width = 8,height = 18)

DotPlot(object_d30, feats, assay = "Spatial", group.by = "first_type")+ 
  theme(axis.title = element_blank()) + custom_theme
ggsave("YWV02_D30PW_OCInducerDotPlot.png", width = 8,height = 18)
```

```{r Use custom feature plot to plot Mac II markers}

feats <- c("Igf1")

feat_colors <- c("purple")
p_uw <- CustomSpatialFeatureCoexpPlot(
  object = object_uw,
  image = "slice1",
  genes = feats,
  gene_colors = feat_colors,
  assay = "Spatial",
  pt.size.expr = 1,
  pt.size.zero = 0.5,
  bg.col       = "lightgrey"
)
p_uw

SpatialFeaturePlot(object_uw, images = "Region2",features = feats, blend = T)

p_d4 <- CustomSpatialFeatureCoexpPlot(
  object = object_d4,
  image = "slice1",
  genes = feats,
  gene_colors = feat_colors,
  assay = "Spatial",
  pt.size.expr = 1,
  pt.size.zero = 0.5,
  bg.col       = "lightgrey"
)
p_d4

p_d7 <- CustomSpatialFeatureCoexpPlot(
  object = object_d7,
  image = "slice1",
  genes = feats,
  gene_colors = feat_colors,
  assay = "Spatial",
  pt.size.expr = 1,
  pt.size.zero = 0.5,
  bg.col       = "lightgrey"
)
p_d7

p_d30 <- CustomSpatialFeatureCoexpPlot(
  object = object_d30,
  image = "slice1",
  genes = feats,
  gene_colors = feat_colors,
  assay = "Spatial",
  pt.size.expr = 1,
  pt.size.zero = 0.5,
  bg.col       = "lightgrey"
)
p_d30

ggsave("YWV01_UW_OClikeInducersFeatPlot.png", plot = p_uw, width = 7, height = 8)
ggsave("YWV02_D30PW_OClikeInducersFeatPlot.png", plot = p_d30, width = 7, height = 8)
ggsave("YWV04_D4PW_OClikeInducersFeatPlot.png", plot = p_d4, width = 7, height = 8)
ggsave("YWV03_D7PW_OClikeInducersFeatPlot.png", plot = p_d7, width = 7, height = 8)
```

```{r Plot markers on FeaturePlot}
# "Ctsk","Mmp12",

feats <- c("Ccl8")

# feats <- c("Itgav","Itgb3")

DefaultAssay(object_uw) <- "Spatial"
DefaultAssay(object_d4) <- "Spatial"
DefaultAssay(object_d7) <- "Spatial"
DefaultAssay(object_d30) <- "Spatial"

low_color = "gray90"
high_color = "red3"

SpatialFeaturePlot(object_uw, images = "slice1", feats) & scale_fill_gradient(low = low_color, high = high_color)
SpatialFeaturePlot(object_d4, images = "slice1", feats) & scale_fill_gradient(low = low_color, high = high_color)
SpatialFeaturePlot(object_d7, images = "slice1", feats) & scale_fill_gradient(low = low_color, high = high_color)
SpatialFeaturePlot(object_d30, images = "slice1", feats) & scale_fill_gradient(low = low_color, high = high_color)
```
```{r}
DotPlot(object_uw, "Igf1", group.by = "first_type")
DotPlot(object_d4, "Igf1", group.by = "first_type")
DotPlot(object_d7, "Ccl8", group.by = "first_type")
DotPlot(object_d30, "Igf1", group.by = "first_type")
```


```{r Loop to save individual feature plots of various markers}
markers <- c("Arg1","Nos2","Vegfa","Acp5","Mrc1","C1qa","Cd163","Igf1","Retnla","Spp1")

# Set default assay for all objects
plot_info <- lapply(plot_info, function(obj) {
  DefaultAssay(obj$object) <- "Spatial"
  return(obj)
})

# Loop through conditions, regions, and markers
for (cond in names(plot_info)) {
  obj <- plot_info[[cond]]$object
  regions <- plot_info[[cond]]$regions
  pt.size <- plot_info[[cond]]$pt.size.factor
  
  for (reg in regions) {
    for (marker in markers) {
      p <- SpatialFeaturePlot(
        object = obj,
        features = marker,
        images = reg,
        pt.size.factor = pt.size * 1.1
      ) +
        ggtitle(paste(cond, reg, marker, sep = " - ")) +
        scale_fill_gradient(low = "gray90", high = "red3")
      
      print(p)
      
      # Optionally save
      ggsave(paste0("plots/", cond, "_", reg, "_", marker,".png"), plot = p, width = 7, height = 7, dpi = 300)
    }
  }
}
```

```{r Save function to plot feature expression with cell type highlight}
source("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Manuscripts/Wound Healing Atlas Paper/Immunome Paper/Custom Functions/plot_spatial_overlay.R")
```

```{r Use custom feature/dimplot to plot expression in a given celltype}
# Define variables
genep <- "Igf1"
slice <- "Macrophages II"

# Generate spatial overlay plots
p1 <- plot_spatial_overlay(object_d7,  gene = genep, highlight_group = c(slice, "D7"))
p2 <- plot_spatial_overlay(object_d4,  gene = genep, highlight_group = c(slice, "D4"))
p3 <- plot_spatial_overlay(object_uw,  gene = genep, highlight_group = c(slice, "UW"))
p4 <- plot_spatial_overlay(object_d30, gene = genep, highlight_group = c(slice, "D30"))

p1
# Save individual plots
ggsave(filename = paste0("spatial_plot_", genep, "_D7_", slice, ".png"),
       plot = p1, width = 5, height = 5, dpi = 300, units = "in")

ggsave(filename = paste0("spatial_plot_", genep, "_D4_", slice, ".png"),
       plot = p2, width = 5, height = 5, dpi = 300, units = "in")

ggsave(filename = paste0("spatial_plot_", genep, "_UW_", slice, ".png"),
       plot = p3, width = 5, height = 5, dpi = 300, units = "in")

ggsave(filename = paste0("spatial_plot_", genep, "_D30_", slice, ".png"),
       plot = p4, width = 5, height = 5, dpi = 300, units = "in")

# Combine plots using patchwork
library(patchwork)
wrap_plot <- (p3 | p1) / (p2 | p4)

# Save combined plot
ggsave(filename = paste0("Spatial_", slice, "_plot_", genep, "_combined.png"),
       plot = wrap_plot, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300, units = "in")
```

```{r Plot coexpression of IGF1 in Macrophage II}
# Define variables
genep <- "Igf1"
slice <- "Macrophages II"

p1 <- plot_spatial_overlay(object_uw,  
                           gene = genep, 
                           highlight_group = c(slice, "UW"),
                           image = "Region2",
                           highlight_color = pal[slice],
                           high_color = "goldenrod1")

p1

ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = "YWV01_UW_MacII_Igf1_CoexpressionPlot", width = 8, height = 8)

p1 <- plot_spatial_overlay(object_uw,  
                           gene = genep, 
                           highlight_group = c(slice, "UW"),
                           image = "ROI",
                           pt.size.highlight = 1.5,
                           pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
                           highlight_color = pal[slice],
                           high_color = "goldenrod1")

p1 + NoLegend() + theme(plot.title = element_blank())
ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = "YWV01_UW_MacII_Igf1_CoexpressionPlot_ROI", width = 7, height = 7)

```
```{r}
# Define variables
genep <- "Thbs1"
slice <- "Macrophages I"

p1 <- plot_spatial_overlay(object_d4,  
                           gene = genep, 
                           highlight_group = c(slice, "D4"),
                           image = "Region1",
                           highlight_color = pal[slice],
                           high_color = "red3")

p1
```

```{r Add OxPhos module score}
celltype_kegg <- read_xlsx("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Manuscripts/Wound Healing Atlas Paper/Immunome Paper/Figure 1/IntegratedImmune_CellMarkers_KEGGResults.xlsx")

pathways <- geneInCategory(celltype_kegg)
# Pull gene IDs associated with OxPhos pathway
genes_mmu00190 <- unlist(lapply(pathways, function(cell) cell[["mmu00190"]]))
genes_mmu00190 <- unique(genes_mmu00190)  # remove duplicates if needed

# Convert IDs back to symbols
genes_mmu00190 <- bitr(genes_mmu00190,
                    fromType = "ENTREZID",
                    toType = "SYMBOL",
                    OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db)

# Use the gene symbols to calculate a module score
for(cond in names(plot_info)){
  obj <- plot_info[[cond]]$object
  
  DefaultAssay(obj) <- "Spatial"
  
  obj <- AddModuleScore(obj, list(genes_mmu00190$SYMBOL), name = "OxPhos Score")
  
  plot_info[[cond]]$object <- obj
  
  rm(obj)
}

SpatialFeaturePlot(plot_info$YWV01_UW$object, "OxPhos Score1",images = "Region2",
                        min.cutoff = c("q5"))  & custom_theme
SpatialFeaturePlot(plot_info$YWV04_D4PW$object, "OxPhos Score1",images = "Region1",
                        min.cutoff = c("q5"))  & custom_theme
SpatialFeaturePlot(plot_info$YWV03_D7PW$object, "OxPhos Score1",images = "Region1",
                        min.cutoff = c("q5"))  & custom_theme
SpatialFeaturePlot(plot_info$YWV02_D30PW$object, "OxPhos Score1",images = "Region1",
                        min.cutoff = c("q5"))  & custom_theme

plot_info <- lapply(plot_info, function(info) {
  obj <- info$object
  Idents(obj) <- "first_type"
  info$object <- obj
  info
})

# Plot expression of OxPhos in immune cells
DotPlot(plot_info$YWV01_UW$object, "OxPhos Score1", idents = immune_types)
DotPlot(plot_info$YWV04_D4PW$object, "OxPhos Score1", idents = immune_types)
DotPlot(plot_info$YWV03_D7PW$object, "OxPhos Score1", idents = immune_types)
DotPlot(plot_info$YWV02_D30PW$object, "OxPhos Score1", idents = immune_types)
```
We can then use the spatial overlay plot to show different cell types
```{r}
genep <- "OxPhos Score1"
slice <- "OC-like cells"

p1 <- plot_spatial_overlay(plot_info$YWV03_D7PW$object,  
                           gene = genep, 
                           highlight_group = c(slice, "D4PW"),
                           image = "Region1",
                           highlight_color = pal[slice],
                           high_color = "goldenrod1",
                           min_expr = 0.25)

p1

```

## Wound Site specific analysis
```{r Add ROI Metadata}
for (cond in names(plot_info)) {
  obj <- plot_info[[cond]]$object
  
  roi_file <- file.path(
    "~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Team/Immunodynamics/Simon Van Deursen/DATA/Spatial Transcriptomics",
    cond, paste0(cond, "_Realignment/ROIs.csv")
  )
  
  # 1. Skip if ROI file doesn't exist
  if (!file.exists(roi_file)) {
    message("Skipping ", cond, " — no ROI file.")
    next
  }
  
  roi.coordinates <- read.csv(roi_file)
  
  # 2. Skip if no barcodes in file
  if (nrow(roi.coordinates) == 0) {
    message("Skipping ", cond, " — ROI file empty.")
    next
  }
  
  roi.assignments <- setNames(roi.coordinates$ROIs, roi.coordinates$Barcode)
  
  # 3. Add metadata only for barcodes that exist in obj
  roi.assignments <- roi.assignments[names(roi.assignments) %in% colnames(obj)]
  if (length(roi.assignments) == 0) {
    message("Skipping ", cond, " — no matching barcodes in object.")
    next
  }
  
  obj <- AddMetaData(obj, roi.assignments, col.name = "ROI")
  
  # 4. Extract coordinates
  coords <- GetTissueCoordinates(obj)
  coords$ROI <- obj$ROI[rownames(coords)]
  
  # 5. Split by ROI — keep even if only 1 ROI present
  region_coords_list <- split(coords, coords$ROI)
  
  # 6. Loop over ROIs and make convex hull overlays
  for (region_name in names(region_coords_list)) {
    region_coords <- region_coords_list[[region_name]]
    
    # Need ≥3 points for convex hull
    if (nrow(region_coords) < 3) {
      message("Skipping ROI ", region_name, " — not enough points.")
      next
    }
    
    hull_indices <- chull(region_coords$x, region_coords$y)
    hull_coords <- region_coords[hull_indices, c("x", "y")]
    hull_coords <- rbind(hull_coords, hull_coords[1, ]) # close polygon
    hull_coords$cell <- region_name
    
    print(hull_coords)  # sanity check
    
    region_segmentation <- CreateSegmentation(hull_coords)
    
    # Show overlay — this won't overwrite the object
    obj[[region_name]] <- Overlay(obj[["slice1"]], region_segmentation)
  }
  
  # Save modified object back into plot_info
  plot_info[[cond]]$object <- obj
}

SpatialDimPlot(object = plot_info$YWV01_UW$object, 
               group.by = "first_type", pt.size.factor = 3, images = "ROI")

SpatialDimPlot(object = plot_info$YWV03_D7PW$object, 
               group.by = "banksy_cluster_0.5", pt.size.factor = 3, images = "ROI1")
```
```{r Add Wound Edge ROI to D7PW dataset}
cond <- "YWV03_D7PW"

roi_file <- "~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Sequencing Repository/Data/Spatial Data/YWV03_D7PW/YWV03_D7PW_Realignment/WoundEdgeROI.csv"

roi.coordinates <- read.csv(roi_file)

roi.assignments <- setNames(roi.coordinates$WoundEdgeROI, roi.coordinates$Barcode)
  
# 3. Add metadata only for barcodes that exist in obj
  roi.assignments <- roi.assignments[names(roi.assignments) %in% colnames(object_d7)]
  if (length(roi.assignments) == 0) {
    message("Skipping ", cond, " — no matching barcodes in object.")
    next
  }
  
  object_d7 <- AddMetaData(object_d7, roi.assignments, col.name = "WoundEdgeROI")
  
  # 4. Extract coordinates
  coords <- GetTissueCoordinates(object_d7)
  coords$ROI <- object_d7$WoundEdgeROI[rownames(coords)]
  
  # 5. Split by ROI — keep even if only 1 ROI present
  region_coords_list <- split(coords, coords$ROI)
  
  # 6. Loop over ROIs and make convex hull overlays
  for (region_name in names(region_coords_list)) {
    region_coords <- region_coords_list[[region_name]]
    
    # Need ≥3 points for convex hull
    if (nrow(region_coords) < 3) {
      message("Skipping ROI ", region_name, " — not enough points.")
      next
    }
    
    hull_indices <- chull(region_coords$x, region_coords$y)
    hull_coords <- region_coords[hull_indices, c("x", "y")]
    hull_coords <- rbind(hull_coords, hull_coords[1, ]) # close polygon
    hull_coords$cell <- region_name
    
    print(hull_coords)  # sanity check
    
    region_segmentation <- CreateSegmentation(hull_coords)
    
    # Show overlay — this won't overwrite the object
    object_d7[[region_name]] <- Overlay(object_d7[["slice1"]], region_segmentation)
  }

SpatialDimPlot(object = object_d7, 
               group.by = "banksy_cluster_0.5", pt.size.factor = 3, images = "WoundEdgeROI")
```

Save plots of ROI clusters
```{r Loop to save plots of Wound site ROI}
# Define images and point sizes for each sample
plot_settings <- list(
  "YWV01_UW" = list(image = "ROI", pt_size = 1.5),
    "YWV04_D4PW" = list(image = "ROI1", pt_size = 1.5),
  "YWV03_D7PW" = list(image = "ROI1", pt_size = 1.5),
  "YWV02_D30PW" = list(image = "ROI", pt_size = 1.5)
)

for (cond in names(plot_info)) {
  if (!cond %in% names(plot_settings)) {
    message("Skipping ", cond, " (not in plot list).")
    next
  }
  
  obj <- plot_info[[cond]]$object
  cluster_col <- "banksy_cluster_0.5"
  
  # Get cluster levels
  if (is.factor(obj@meta.data[[cluster_col]])) {
    cluster_levels <- levels(obj@meta.data[[cluster_col]])
  } else {
    cluster_levels <- sort(unique(as.character(obj@meta.data[[cluster_col]])))
  }
  
  cluster_colors <- setNames(viridis(length(cluster_levels), option = "H"), cluster_levels)
  
  image_to_plot <- plot_settings[[cond]]$image
  pt_size <- plot_settings[[cond]]$pt_size
  
  p <- SpatialDimPlot(obj,
                      group.by = cluster_col,
                      pt.size.factor = pt_size,
                      images = image_to_plot,
                      cols = cluster_colors) & custom_theme +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
  
  ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(cond, "_ROI_ClusterPlot"),
         plot = p,
         width = 8, height = 7)
  
   p <- SpatialDimPlot(obj,
                      group.by = cluster_col,
                      pt.size.factor = pt_size,
                      images = image_to_plot,
                      label = T,repel = T,label.size = 2,label.box = T,
                      cols = cluster_colors) & custom_theme +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
  
  ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(cond, "_ROI_ClusterPlot_Labeled"),
         plot = p,
         width = 8, height = 7)
  
    p <- SpatialDimPlot(obj,
                      group.by = "metaclusters",
                      pt.size.factor = pt_size,
                      images = image_to_plot,
                      cols = metacluster_colors) + NoLegend()  & custom_theme +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
  
  ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = paste0(cond, "_ROI_MetaclusterPlot"),
         plot = p,
         width = 7, height = 7)
}
```
Save plots of unwounded immune cells in the ROI for figure 1
```{r Unwounded ROI Immune highlight for Fig 1}
p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
  seurat_obj = plot_info[["YWV01_UW"]]$object,
  region = "ROI",
  annotation_col = "first_type",
  cells_highlight = immune_types,
  pt.size.highlight = 1.5,
  pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
  stroke.size = 0.05,
  pal = pal,
  custom_theme = custom_theme
) + NoLegend()
print(p)

ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = "YWV01_UW_ROI_ImmuneHighlight", width = 7, height = 7)

SpatialDimPlot( plot_info[["YWV01_UW"]]$object, group.by = "AnatomicalSite",
                images = "ROI", pt.size.factor = 2) & custom_theme +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank()) + NoLegend()

ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = "YWV01_UW_ROI_AnatomicalRegions", width = 7, height = 7)

```
Double check the wound site annotations for each object
```{r Check Wound Site annotations}
unique(object_uw$WoundSite)
unique(object_d4$WoundSite)
unique(object_d7$WoundSite)
unique(object_d30$WoundSite)
```
Examining the unique annotations, we can see the D7 object has some spots missing 
annotations. We can plot where they are in the object and then add an annotation
```{r Examine spots in D7 missing wound site and add annotation}
Idents(object_d7) <- "WoundSite"
missing_cells <-  WhichCells(object_d7, idents = "")
# Plot wound sites
SpatialDimPlot(
      object_d7,
      images = "slice1"
    )
# Plot unannotated cells
SpatialDimPlot(
      object_d7,
      images = "slice1",
      cells.highlight = missing_cells
    )

# Based on the plots, we can see that the annotation for spots missing one depends on the region
object_d7$WoundSite[colnames(object_d7) %in% missing_cells & object_d7$Region == "Region1"] <- "Periphery"
object_d7$WoundSite[colnames(object_d7) %in% missing_cells & object_d7$Region == "Region2"] <- "WoundEdge"

Idents(object_d7) <- "WoundSite"
levels(object_d7) <- c("Wound","WoundEdge","Periphery")
object_d7$WoundSite <- Idents(object_d7)

SpatialDimPlot(
      object_d7,
      images = "slice1"
    )
```
Save DimPlots of woundsite for each object
```{r Save WoundSite annotation Dimplots}
woundsite_pal <- c("Wound" = "#7E1900",
                   "WoundEdge" = "#E5E598",
                   "Periphery" = "#3F85BB")

for (info_name in names(plot_info)) {
  info <- plot_info[[info_name]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  regions <- info$regions
  
  for (region in regions) {
    # Generate plot
    p <- SpatialDimPlot(
      seurat_obj,
      images = region,
      group.by = "WoundSite",
      cols = woundsite_pal
    ) +
      ggtitle(paste(info_name, region, "WoundSite")) &
      custom_theme +
      theme(
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
      ) + NoLegend()
    
    # Save the plot
    ggsave_multidevice(
      filename_base = paste(info_name, region, "WoundSite", sep = "_"),
      plot = p,
      width = 6,
      height = 7
    )
  }
}

```

```{r}
p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
  seurat_obj = object_d7,
  dataset = "YWV03_D7PW",
  region = "ROI1",
  annotation_col = "first_type",
  cells_highlight = c("OC-like cells", "Tregs"),
  pt.size.highlight = 1,
  pt.size.unselected = 0.5,
  stroke.size = 0.05,
  pal = pal,
  custom_theme = custom_theme,
  scalebar_length_um = 100,
  scalebar_position = "bottomright"
) + NoLegend()

print(p)

```

We can also manually annotate wound center in loupe browser, and take that info to 
add to the respective datasets which need it
```{r Add wound center image to D4 and D7PW}
datasets <- c("YWV02_D30PW","YWV03_D7PW","YWV04_D4PW")
for (cond in datasets) {
  
  obj <- plot_info[[cond]]$object
  
  roi_file <- file.path(
    "~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Sequencing Repository/Data/Spatial Data/",
    cond, paste0(cond, "_Realignment/WoundSiteROI.csv")
  )

  # 1. Skip if ROI file doesn't exist
  if (!file.exists(roi_file)) {
    message("Skipping ", cond, " — no ROI file.")
    next
  }
  
  roi.coordinates <- read.csv(roi_file)
  
  # 2. Skip if no barcodes in file
  if (nrow(roi.coordinates) == 0) {
    message("Skipping ", cond, " — ROI file empty.")
    next
  }
  
  roi.assignments <- setNames(roi.coordinates$WoundSiteROI, roi.coordinates$Barcode)
  
  # 3. Add metadata only for barcodes that exist in obj
  roi.assignments <- roi.assignments[names(roi.assignments) %in% colnames(obj)]
  if (length(roi.assignments) == 0) {
    message("Skipping ", cond, " — no matching barcodes in object.")
    next
  }
  
  obj <- AddMetaData(obj, roi.assignments, col.name = "WoundSiteROI")
  
  # 4. Extract coordinates
  coords <- GetTissueCoordinates(obj)
  coords$WoundSiteROI <- obj$WoundSiteROI[rownames(coords)]
  
  # 5. Split by ROI — keep even if only 1 ROI present
  region_coords_list <- split(coords, coords$WoundSiteROI)
  
  # 6. Loop over ROIs and make convex hull overlays
  for (region_name in names(region_coords_list)) {
    region_coords <- region_coords_list[[region_name]]
    
    # Need ≥3 points for convex hull
    if (nrow(region_coords) < 3) {
      message("Skipping ROI ", region_name, " — not enough points.")
      next
    }
    
    hull_indices <- chull(region_coords$x, region_coords$y)
    hull_coords <- region_coords[hull_indices, c("x", "y")]
    hull_coords <- rbind(hull_coords, hull_coords[1, ]) # close polygon
    hull_coords$cell <- region_name
    
    print(hull_coords)  # sanity check
    
    region_segmentation <- CreateSegmentation(hull_coords)
    
    # Show overlay — this won't overwrite the object
    obj[[region_name]] <- Overlay(obj[["slice1"]], region_segmentation)
  }
  
  # Save modified object back into plot_info
  plot_info[[cond]]$object <- obj
}

SpatialDimPlot(object = plot_info$YWV04_D4PW$object, 
               group.by = "first_type", pt.size.factor = 3, images = "WoundCenter1") + NoLegend()

SpatialDimPlot(object = plot_info$YWV03_D7PW$object, 
               group.by = "first_type", pt.size.factor = 3, images = "WoundEdge1") + NoLegend()
```

With wound center added, we can save plots highlighting immune cells 
```{r Loop to save ROI plots for wounded timepoints}
setwd("~/SRSP Laboratory Dropbox/SRSP Lab/Resources/Town Square - Data Share/Manuscripts/Wound Healing Atlas Paper/Immunome Paper/Figure 3 Spatial/Anatomical Sites")

# Define datasets and ROIs
datasets <- c("YWV02_D30PW", "YWV03_D7PW", "YWV04_D4PW")
rois     <- c("WoundCenter1", "WoundEdge1", "Periphery1")

# Collect all possible levels across all datasets
all_sites_global <- unique(unlist(
  lapply(datasets, function(cond) {
    unique(plot_info[[cond]]$object$AnatomicalSite)
  })
))

all_sites_global <- factor(all_sites_global, 
                           levels = sort(all_sites_global))

# Build a single consistent palette across datasets
site_pal_global <- setNames(
  hue_pal()(length(all_sites_global)),
  levels(all_sites_global)
)

# Loop through datasets and ROIs
for (cond in datasets) {
  
  obj <- plot_info[[cond]]$object
  
  # Subset palette to only sites present in this object
  site_pal <- site_pal_global[names(site_pal_global) %in% unique(obj$AnatomicalSite)]
  
  for (roi in rois) {
    # Skip if ROI overlay not present
    if (!(roi %in% names(obj))) {
      message("Skipping ", cond, " — no overlay for ", roi)
      next
    }
    
    # Make plot highlighting immune cells 
    # p <- CustomSpatialHighlightPlot(
    #   seurat_obj        = obj,
    #   region            = roi,
    #   annotation_col    = "first_type",
    #   cells_highlight   = immune_types,
    #   pt.size.highlight = 2,
    #   pt.size.unselected= 1,
    #   stroke.size       = 0.05,
    #   pal               = pal,
    #   custom_theme      = custom_theme
    # ) + NoLegend()
    # 
    # print(p)
    # 
    # # Save with dataset and ROI in filename
    # outname <- paste0(cond, "_", roi, "_ImmuneHighlight")
    # ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = outname, width = 7, height = 7)
    
    # Make plot showing anatomical sites
    p <- SpatialDimPlot(
      object = obj,
      images = roi,
      group.by = "AnatomicalSite", 
      pt.size.factor = 3.75,
      cols = site_pal) & 
      custom_theme +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank()) 
    
    print(p) + NoLegend()
    
    # Save with dataset and ROI in filename
    outname <- paste0(cond, "_", roi, "_AnatomicalSites")
    ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = outname, width = 7, height = 7)
    
    p <- SpatialDimPlot(
      object = obj,
      images = "slice1",
      group.by = "AnatomicalSite", 
      pt.size.factor = 2.5,
      cols = site_pal) & 
      custom_theme +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank()) 
    
    print(p)
    
    # Save with dataset and ROI in filename
    outname <- paste0(cond, "_", roi, "_AnatomicalSitesLegend")
    ggsave_multidevice(filename_base = outname, width = 7, height = 7)
  }
}

```
We can then take some of these highly expressed genes and plot feature plots
```{r Save feature plots}
# Save wound site associated features
feats <- c("Timp1","Srgn","Tnc","Spp1","Cfd","Car3","Ckm")

# Set default assay for all objects
plot_info <- lapply(plot_info, function(obj) {
  DefaultAssay(obj$object) <- "Spatial"
  return(obj)
})

# Loop through conditions, regions, and markers
for (cond in names(plot_info)) {
  obj <- plot_info[[cond]]$object
  regions <- plot_info[[cond]]$regions
  pt.size <- plot_info[[cond]]$pt.size.factor
  
  for (reg in regions) {
    for (marker in feats) {
      p <- SpatialFeaturePlot(
        object = obj,
        features = marker,
        images = reg,
        pt.size.factor = pt.size * 1.1
      ) +
        ggtitle(paste(cond, reg, marker, sep = " - ")) +
        scale_fill_gradient(low = "gray90", high = "red3") &
        custom_theme
      
      print(p)
      
      # Optionally save
      ggsave(paste0("WoundSite Marker FeatPlots/", cond, "_", reg, "_", marker,".png"), plot = p, width = 7, height = 7, dpi = 300)
    }
  }
}
```
We can also use the coexpression function to plot multiple genes at once
```{r Use coexpression featureplot to show wound site markers}
feats <- c("Timp1","Car3")
feat_colors <-  c("#7E1900", "#3F85BB")

p_uw <- CustomSpatialFeatureCoexpPlot(
  object = object_uw,
  image = "slice1",
  genes = feats,
  gene_colors = feat_colors,
  assay = "Spatial",
  pt.size.expr = 1,
  pt.size.zero = 0.5,
  bg.col       = "lightgrey"
)
p_uw

p_d4 <- CustomSpatialFeatureCoexpPlot(
  object = object_d4,
  image = "slice1",
  genes = feats,
  gene_colors = feat_colors,
  assay = "Spatial",
  pt.size.expr = 1,
  pt.size.zero = 0.5,
  bg.col       = "lightgrey"
)
p_d4

p_d7 <- CustomSpatialFeatureCoexpPlot(
  object = object_d7,
  image = "slice1",
  genes = feats,
  gene_colors = feat_colors,
  assay = "Spatial",
  pt.size.expr = 1,
  pt.size.zero = 0.5,
  bg.col       = "lightgrey"
)
p_d7

p_d30 <- CustomSpatialFeatureCoexpPlot(
  object = object_d30,
  image = "slice1",
  genes = feats,
  gene_colors = feat_colors,
  assay = "Spatial",
  pt.size.expr = 1,
  pt.size.zero = 0.5,
  bg.col       = "lightgrey"
)
p_d30

ggsave("YWV01_UW_WoundPeripheryFeatPlot.png", plot = p_uw, width = 7, height = 8)
ggsave("YWV02_D30PW_WoundPeripheryFeatPlot.png", plot = p_d30, width = 7, height = 8)
ggsave("YWV04_D4PW_WoundPeripheryFeatPlot.png", plot = p_d4, width = 7, height = 8)
ggsave("YWV03_D7PW_WoundPeripheryFeatPlot.png", plot = p_d7, width = 7, height = 8)
```
### Marker identification 
To characterize clusters which are in regions of interest, we will first find 
markers of each cluster vs the rest
```{r Find markers for BANKSY clusters}
# UW
uw_cluster_marks <- FindAllMarkers(object_uw, assay = "Spatial", only.pos = T,
                                   group.by = "banksy_cluster_0.5", verbose = F)
# D4PW
d4_cluster_marks <- FindAllMarkers(object_d4, assay = "Spatial", only.pos = T,
                                   group.by = "banksy_cluster_0.5", verbose = F)
# D7PW
d7_cluster_marks <- FindAllMarkers(object_d7, assay = "Spatial", only.pos = T,
                                   group.by = "banksy_cluster_0.5", verbose = F)
# D30PW
d30_cluster_marks <- FindAllMarkers(object_d30, assay = "Spatial",  only.pos = T,
                                   group.by = "banksy_cluster_0.5", verbose = F)
```
Examine BANKSY cluster markers in dotplot
```{r Plot DotPlots of BANKSY markers}
top_marks <- uw_cluster_marks %>%
  filter(avg_log2FC > 1 & p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_head(n=5) %>%
  pull(gene)
  
DotPlot(object_uw, unique(top_marks), group.by = "banksy_cluster_0.5", cols = "RdYlBu",
        assay = "Spatial") +
  geom_point(
    aes(size = pct.exp),
    shape = 21,         # filled circle
    color = "black",    # black outline
    stroke = 0.5
  ) + 
  labs(y = "BANKSY Cluster")+
  guides(
    fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Average\nExpression"),
    size = guide_legend(title = "Percent\nExpressed")
  ) + 
 # theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1.1),
    axis.title.x = element_blank()) + custom_theme
```

```{r Loop to save BANKSY cluster marker dotplots}
# Save DE results as a list
de_results <- list("YWV01_UW" = uw_cluster_marks,
                   "YWV02_D30PW" = d30_cluster_marks,
                   "YWV03_D7PW" = d7_cluster_marks,
                   "YWV04_D4PW" = d4_cluster_marks)

# Loop over datasets to save dotplots
for(dataset in names(plot_info)){
  info <- plot_info[[dataset]]
  seurat_obj <- info$object
  cluster_marks <- de_results[[dataset]]
  
  top_marks <- cluster_marks %>%
  filter(avg_log2FC > 1 & p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_head(n=5) %>%
  pull(gene)
  
  p <- DotPlot(seurat_obj, unique(top_marks), group.by = "banksy_cluster_0.5", 
             cols = "RdYlBu", assay = "Spatial") +
      geom_point(
        aes(size = pct.exp),
        shape = 21,
        color = "black",
        stroke = 0.5) +
        labs(y = "BANKSY Cluster") + 
    guides(
      fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Average\nExpression"),
      size = guide_legend(title = "Percent\nExpressed")
      ) +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1.1),
        axis.title.x = element_blank()
      ) +
      custom_theme
  
  ggsave(paste0(dataset,"_BANKSYClusters_DotPlot.png"),plot = p,
         width = 15, height = 6)
}
```
## Find Wound-associated clusters
We are also interested in leveraging the spatial clusterings to identify wound-associated cellular niches.
To do so, we can quantify the representation of each banksy cluster across different
wound sites to determine which clusters are associated with various regions
```{r Determine BANKSY clusters found primarily in Wound site}
# Save function to determine proportion of cells found in wound site
get_wound_prop <- function(obj, cluster_col = "banksy_cluster_0.5", wound_col = "WoundSite") {
  tab <- table(obj@meta.data[[cluster_col]], obj@meta.data[[wound_col]])
  wound_levels <- colnames(tab)
  
  if ("Wound" %in% wound_levels) {
    counts_wound <- tab[, "Wound"]
  } else {
    counts_wound <- rep(0, nrow(tab))
    names(counts_wound) <- rownames(tab)
  }
  
  prop <- counts_wound / rowSums(tab)
  prop[is.na(prop)] <- 0
  data.frame(cluster = names(prop), prop_in_wound = prop)
}

results <- lapply(names(plot_info), function(name) {
  obj <- plot_info[[name]]$object
  df <- get_wound_prop(obj)
  df$sample <- name
  df
})

results_df <- do.call(rbind, results)

# Filter clusters with majority of spots in Wound
wound_clusters <- subset(results_df, prop_in_wound > 0.5)

# Split wound clusters by sample for downstream applications
wound_clusters <- split(wound_clusters$cluster, wound_clusters$sample)

# For D30PW, add clusters 0 and 1
wound_clusters$YWV02_D30PW <- c("0","1",wound_clusters$YWV02_D30PW)
```
We can also be less stringent in our criteria
```{r Determine BANKSY clusters with any number of cells in wound}
# Function to get clusters with ANY cells in "Wound"
get_clusters_with_wound <- function(obj, cluster_col = "banksy_cluster_0.5", wound_col = "WoundSite") {
  # Skip objects with no "Wound" at all
  if (!"Wound" %in% obj@meta.data[[wound_col]]) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  tab <- table(obj@meta.data[[cluster_col]], obj@meta.data[[wound_col]])
  
  # Get clusters where Wound count > 0
  counts_wound <- tab[, "Wound"]
  clusters_in_wound <- names(counts_wound[counts_wound > 0])
  
  data.frame(cluster = clusters_in_wound)
}

# Apply to all samples
results <- lapply(names(plot_info), function(name) {
  obj <- plot_info[[name]]$object
  clusters_df <- get_clusters_with_wound(obj)
  
  if (!is.null(clusters_df)) {
    clusters_df$sample <- name
    return(clusters_df)
  }
  
  NULL
})

# Remove NULLs and combine
results_df <- do.call(rbind, results)

# Split by sample
wound_clusters <- split(results_df$cluster, results_df$sample)
```
And we can find clusters which are found in wound and wound edge
```{r Find clusters primarily in Wound OR WoundEdge}
get_wound_and_edge_prop <- function(obj, cluster_col = "banksy_cluster_0.5", wound_col = "WoundSite",
                                   top_n = 3) {
  tab <- table(obj@meta.data[[cluster_col]], obj@meta.data[[wound_col]])
  wound_levels <- colnames(tab)
  clusters <- rownames(tab)

  # Safely get counts for Wound and WoundEdge (0 if missing)
  counts_wound <- if ("Wound" %in% wound_levels) tab[, "Wound"] else rep(0, nrow(tab))
  counts_edge  <- if ("WoundEdge" %in% wound_levels) tab[, "WoundEdge"] else rep(0, nrow(tab))
  names(counts_wound) <- clusters
  names(counts_edge) <- clusters

  # Calculate proportions per cluster
  prop_wound <- counts_wound / rowSums(tab)
  prop_edge <- counts_edge / rowSums(tab)
  prop_wound[is.na(prop_wound)] <- 0
  prop_edge[is.na(prop_edge)] <- 0

  # Identify top N clusters in Wound and WoundEdge by absolute counts
  top_wound_clusters <- names(sort(counts_wound, decreasing = TRUE))[1:top_n]
  top_edge_clusters <- names(sort(counts_edge, decreasing = TRUE))[1:top_n]

  # Logical vector: TRUE if cluster is in top N of both Wound and WoundEdge
  top_both <- clusters %in% intersect(top_wound_clusters, top_edge_clusters)

  data.frame(
    cluster = clusters,
    count_wound = counts_wound,
    prop_in_wound = prop_wound,
    count_edge = counts_edge,
    prop_in_edge = prop_edge,
    top_in_both = top_both
  )
}

# Usage example:
results <- lapply(names(plot_info), function(name) {
  obj <- plot_info[[name]]$object
  df <- get_wound_and_edge_prop(obj)
  df$sample <- name
  df
})

results_df <- do.call(rbind, results)

# Filter clusters with majority of spots in Wound
wound_edge_clusters <- subset(results_df, prop_in_wound > 0.5 | prop_in_edge > 0.5)

# Split wound clusters by sample for downstream applications
wound_edge_clusters <- split(wound_edge_clusters$cluster, wound_edge_clusters$sample)
```
We can examine where these clusters are in each object
```{r Save plots of wound site clusters}
# Loop to save plots of wound-site clusters for each object which has them
for(cond in names(wound_clusters)){
  # Isolate objects and clusters for each condition
  obj <- plot_info[[cond]]$object
  clusters <- wound_clusters[[cond]]
  regions <- plot_info[[cond]]$regions
  pt.size <- plot_info[[cond]]$pt.size.factor
  
  Idents(obj) <- "banksy_cluster_0.5"
  # Save color palette for each condition
  cluster_levels <- levels(obj)
  cluster_colors <- setNames(viridis(length(cluster_levels), option = "H"), cluster_levels)
  cluster_colors <- cluster_colors[clusters]
  cluster_colors <- c(cluster_colors, Unselected = "grey90")
  
  cells <- CellsByIdentities(obj, idents = clusters)
  
   for (region in regions) {
      p <- SpatialDimPlot(obj,
                          images = region,
                          cells.highlight = cells, 
                          cols.highlight = cluster_colors) &
      custom_theme +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
      
      ggsave(paste0(cond, "_WoundClusters_", region,".png"), width = 8, height = 7)
   }
}
```

We can then take the list of wound clusters and determine the cell types composing them
Quantify fine cell type representation within each specified cluster
```{r Quantify fine cell types in clusters of interest}
# Loop through each relevant condition
for (cond in names(wound_clusters)) {
  seurat_obj <- plot_info[[cond]]$object
  clusters_of_interest <- wound_clusters[[cond]]

  # Set identity class to clusters
  Idents(seurat_obj) <- "banksy_cluster_0.5"

  # Subset cells from the desired clusters
  selected_cells <- WhichCells(seurat_obj, idents = clusters_of_interest)

  # Get metadata and count first_type representation
  meta_sub <- seurat_obj@meta.data[selected_cells, ]

  summary_df <- meta_sub %>%
    dplyr::count(banksy_cluster_0.5, first_type) %>%
    group_by(banksy_cluster_0.5) %>%
    mutate(proportion = n / sum(n)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    filter(proportion >= 0.05)  # Filter out cell types < 5% in their cluster

p <-  ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = reorder(first_type, -proportion), y = proportion, fill = first_type)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "black") +
    facet_wrap(~banksy_cluster_0.5, scales = "free_y") +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = cell_colors) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "none") +
    labs(
      y = "Proportion of Cluster"
    ) + theme(
      strip.text = element_text(size = 18), color = "black",
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
      axis.title.y = element_blank(),
      panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black")
    ) + custom_theme

print(p)

  # Save plot
  ggsave(filename = paste0(cond,"ROICluster_CellTypeQuant.png"),
         plot = p, width = 16, height = 8)
}
```
We can also try to manipulate plotting features to make plots more consistent
```{r}
ncol_fixed <- 4  # Same as before
base_facet_height <- 2.5  # Height per facet row in inches (adjust as you like)

for (cond in names(wound_clusters)) {
  seurat_obj <- plot_info[[cond]]$object
  clusters_of_interest <- wound_clusters[[cond]]

  Idents(seurat_obj) <- "banksy_cluster_0.5"
  selected_cells <- WhichCells(seurat_obj, idents = clusters_of_interest)

  meta_sub <- seurat_obj@meta.data[selected_cells, ]

  summary_df <- meta_sub %>%
    dplyr::count(banksy_cluster_0.5, first_type) %>%
    group_by(banksy_cluster_0.5) %>%
    mutate(proportion = n / sum(n)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    filter(proportion >= 0.05, first_type != "Unknown")

  p <- ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = reorder(first_type, -proportion),
                              y = proportion,
                              fill = first_type)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "black") +
    facet_wrap(~banksy_cluster_0.5, scales = "free_y", ncol = ncol_fixed) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = cell_colors) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "none") +
    labs(y = "Proportion of Cluster") +
    theme(
      strip.text = element_text(size = 18, color = "black"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14, color = "black"),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
      axis.title.y = element_blank(),
      panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black")
    ) +
    custom_theme

  print(p)

  # Calculate how many rows of facets this plot will have
  n_clusters <- length(clusters_of_interest)
  n_rows <- ceiling(n_clusters / ncol_fixed)

  # Set height proportional to number of rows
  plot_height <- n_rows * base_facet_height

  ggsave(filename = paste0(cond, "ROICluster_CellTypeQuant.png"),
         plot = p,
         width = 16,  # your fixed width
         height = plot_height)
}

```
Next, find markers of these different populations and save plots
```{r Find markers for wound-specific clusters}
# Loop to find markers for clusters
for(cond in names(wound_clusters)){
  
  obj <- plot_info[[cond]]$object
  clusters <- wound_clusters[[cond]]
  
  Idents(obj) <- "banksy_cluster_0.5"
  # Save color palette for each condition
  cluster_levels <- levels(obj)
  cluster_colors <- setNames(viridis(length(cluster_levels), option = "H"), cluster_levels)
  cluster_colors <- cluster_colors[clusters]
  
  # Subset to only keep woundsite clusters
  obj <- subset(obj, idents = clusters)
  
  # Find markers for said clusters
  cluster_marks <- FindAllMarkers(obj, assay = "Spatial", 
                                  only.pos = T, 
                                  subset.ident = clusters,
                                  group.by = "banksy_cluster_0.5", 
                                  verbose = F)
  
  # Select top 5 genes per cluster
  top_marks <- cluster_marks %>%
  filter(avg_log2FC > 1 & p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_head(n=10) %>%
  pull(gene)
  
  obj <- ScaleData(obj, assay = "Spatial", features = top_marks)
  
  p <- DoHeatmap(obj,
          features = top_marks,
          group.by = "banksy_cluster_0.5",
          group.colors = cluster_colors, 
          assay = "Spatial") +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low = "blue",
      mid = "white",
      high = "red",
      midpoint = 0
    ) + theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10)
    ) & custom_theme

    ggsave(paste0(cond,"_WoundSiteClusters_Heatmap.png"),plot = p,
         width = 14, height = 8)
    
  # p <- DotPlot(obj, unique(top_marks), group.by = "banksy_cluster_0.5", 
  #            cols = "RdYlBu", assay = "Spatial") +
  #     geom_point(
  #       aes(size = pct.exp),
  #       shape = 21,
  #       color = "black",
  #       stroke = 0.5) +
  #       labs(y = "BANKSY Cluster") + 
  #   guides(
  #     fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Average\nExpression"),
  #     size = guide_legend(title = "Percent\nExpressed")
  #     ) +
  #     theme(
  #       axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1.1),
  #       axis.title.x = element_blank()
  #     ) +
  #     custom_theme
  
  # ggsave(paste0(cond,"_WoundSiteClusters_DotPlot.png"),plot = p,
  #        width = 15, height = 6)
  
}

```

With markers for each cluster identified, we can select only the relevant clusters
for region-specific analysis
```{r Select clusters in regions of interest and plot markers}
# Save cluster lists
d4_clusters <- c("1","2","5","6","8","12","13","14","15","20","21","22")

d7_clusters <- c("1","2","3","10","11","13","17","18","21")

d30_clusters <- c("1","2","6","11","16","17","18","19","22")

# D4PW
d4_cluster_marks1 <- FindAllMarkers(object_d4, assay = "Spatial", only.pos = T, subset.ident = d4_clusters,
                                   group.by = "alignment_clusters", verbose = F)

# Filter marker lists 
d4_cluster_marks_filtered <- d4_cluster_marks %>%
  filter(p_val_adj < 0.05 & cluster %in% d4_clusters) %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
  
# d4_cluster_marks_filtered <- d4_cluster_marks %>%
#   filter(p_val_adj < 0.05, cluster %in% d4_clusters) %>%
#   group_by(gene) %>%
#   filter(n() == 1) %>%
#   group_by(cluster) %>%
#   slice_head(n = 5) %>%
#   ungroup()

# Plot marker genes on DotPlots
Idents(object_d4) <- "alignment_clusters"
DotPlot(object_d4, unique(d4_cluster_marks_filtered$gene), idents = d4_clusters) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
We can also subset the objects and find markers across each of the wound-associated clusters
```{r Subset D4 and find ROI markers}
Idents(object_d4) <- "alignment_clusters"
object_d4_subset <- subset(object_d4, idents = d4_clusters)

d4_cluster_marks <- FindAllMarkers(object_d4_subset, assay = "Spatial", only.pos = T,
                                   group.by = "alignment_clusters", verbose = F)

d4_cluster_marks_filtered <- d4_cluster_marks %>%
  filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_head(n = 5)

DotPlot(object_d4, unique(d4_cluster_marks_filtered$gene), idents = d4_clusters,
        cols = "RdYlBu") +
  geom_point(aes(size=pct.exp), shape = 21, colour="black", stroke=0.25) +
  guides(color = guide_colorbar(title = "Average\nExpression"),
```
